blocks:
Ayahs

الٓمٓ (۱)
Alif, Lam, Meem. (1)
أَحَسِبَ ٱلنَّاسُ أَن يُتْرَكُوٓا۟ أَن يَقُولُوٓا۟ ءَامَنَّا وَهُمْ لَا يُفْتَنُونَ (۲)
Do the people suppose that they will be let off because they say, ‘We have faith,’ and they will not be tested? (2)
وَلَقَدْ فَتَنَّا ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۖ فَلَيَعْلَمَنَّ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِينَ صَدَقُوا۟ وَلَيَعْلَمَنَّ ٱلْكَـٰذِبِينَ (۳)
Certainly We tested those who were before them. So Allah shall surely ascertain those who are truthful, and He shall surely ascertain the liars. (3)

Key Points

| The word «فتنة» means melting gold to separate its impurities. Because the essence of a person is separated from false slogans during hardships and difficulties, events and trials are called «فتنه».

Messages

1- Faith is not merely by tongue and slogan; rather, it is accompanied by trials. «أحسب... هم لایفتنون» (Claiming is not enough; one must observe and judge based on performance.)

Sa'di, although you are eloquent and a counselor, Success comes through action, not through eloquence.

2- Testing is a Divine tradition throughout history. «ولقد فتنّا الّذین من قبلهم»

3- We should not consider events to be coincidences; they are all means for our testing. «فتنّا»

4- Familiarity with the history and events of previous generations prepares people to accept current events. «من قبلهم»

5- The reason for Divine tests is the realization and manifestation of Allah’s eternal knowledge, the separation of true believers, and the blossoming and actualization of inner potentials. «فلیعلمنّ... الّذین صدقوا و... الكاذبین»

Explanations

| Portrait of Surah Al-Ankabut (The Spider) This Surah was revealed in Mecca and has sixty-nine verses. The names of some Surahs of the Quran, such as Al-Baqarah (The Cow), Al-Fil (The Elephant), An-Naml (The Ant), and An-Nahl (The Bee), are derived from the names of animals mentioned in them in some way. In Ayah 41 of this Surah, the foundation of polytheism (shirk) is considered weak and unstable, like the house of a spider, and thus this Surah is named Al-Ankabut (The Spider). This Surah discusses several topics, including: faith, human duty (taklif), Divine testing, the history of some prophets, the prohibition of improper argumentation, and reliance upon none but Allah.

Ayahs

أَمْ حَسِبَ ٱلَّذِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ ٱلسَّيِّـَٔاتِ أَن يَسْبِقُونَا ۚ سَآءَ مَا يَحْكُمُونَ (٤)
Do those who commit misdeeds suppose that they can outmaneuver Us? Evil is the judgement that they make. (4)
مَن كَانَ يَرْجُوا۟ لِقَآءَ ٱللَّهِ فَإِنَّ أَجَلَ ٱللَّهِ لَـَٔاتٍ ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ (٥)
Whoever expects to encounter Allah [should know that] Allah’s [appointed] time will indeed come, and He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing. (5)

Key Points

Imam Ali عليه السلام said: "The meaning of 'meeting Allah' (لقاء اللّه) is the Day of Resurrection."(109)

109) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn and Tawhid al-Saduq, p. 267.

Messages

1- Committing sins and repeating them affects a person's insight and makes him delusional (or prone to wishful thinking). «أم حسب الّذین یعملون السّیئات»

2- We should cure delusions and wishful thinking by remembering death and the Resurrection. Both the believers who are undergoing trials and the transgressors should know that opportunities are finite, and Allah's mercy and wrath will certainly reach them. «أحسب النّاس أن یتركوا ... أم حسب الّذین یعملون السّیئات ... من كان یرجوا لقاء اللّه...»

3- Neither those who claim faith are exempt from divine trial, nor are the transgressors exempt from divine retribution. «یسبقونا» (The transgressors imagine that the hand of Allah cannot reach them.)

4- False thoughts and baseless assumptions must be condemned. «ساء ما یحكمون»

5- Allah hears our words when we declare faith, and He also knows our thoughts and inner states. «و هو السمیع العلیم»

Ayah

وَمَن جَـٰهَدَ فَإِنَّمَا يُجَـٰهِدُ لِنَفْسِهِۦٓ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَغَنِىٌّ عَنِ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (٦)
Whoever strives, strives only for his own sake. Indeed Allah has no need of the creatures. (6)

Key Points

The meaning of Jihad (striving) in this Ayah is not limited only to fighting with the sword and against enemies, but rather it refers to every kind of effort and striving; whether this effort is for self-purification (self-building), known as Jihad al-Nafs (struggle against the self), or fighting against the whispers of Satan, or against external enemies.

Messages

1- Allah does not need our striving. «و مَن جاهد فانّما یجاهد لنفسه»

I did not create so that I might gain profit, But rather so that I might bestow generosity upon the servants.

2- To encourage people toward positive efforts, we should utilize innate and natural inclinations, such as self-love (hubb al-nafs). (Man is naturally seeking his own profit and benefit.) «و مَن جاهد فانّما یجاهد لنفسه»

3- Allah's independence (self-sufficiency) is real and absolute. (In contrast to our declaration of independence, which is made despite our need.) «انّ اللَّه لغنىّ عن العالمین»

4- Allah is independent both of humans, and of angels, and of all other beings. «لَغنىّ عن العالَمین»

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ لَنُكَفِّرَنَّ عَنْهُمْ سَيِّـَٔاتِهِمْ وَلَنَجْزِيَنَّهُمْ أَحْسَنَ ٱلَّذِى كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۷)
As for those who have faith and do righteous deeds, We will absolve them of their misdeeds and We will surely reward them by the best of what they used to do. (7)

Key Points

Among the best examples of Jihad (struggle) that we read about in the previous Ayah are faith (Iman) and righteous deeds ('Amal al-Salih), which are mentioned in this Ayah.

Messages

1- Man requires both faith and righteous deeds to receive Divine rewards. «آمنوا و عملوا الصّالحات»

2- Believers are not immune to slips and sins. «و الّذین آمنوا... لنكفّرن عنهم سیئاتهم»

3- The Divine reward is not only the forgiveness of past sins, but also the reception of the best reward. «لنكفّرن عنهم سیئاتهم و لنجزینّهم»

4- The reception of the reward occurs after being purified from sins. «لنكفّرن... لنجزینّهم»

Ayah

وَوَصَّيْنَا ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ بِوَٰلِدَيْهِ حُسْنًا ۖ وَإِن جَـٰهَدَاكَ لِتُشْرِكَ بِى مَا لَيْسَ لَكَ بِهِۦ عِلْمٌ فَلَا تُطِعْهُمَآ ۚ إِلَىَّ مَرْجِعُكُمْ فَأُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (۸)
We have enjoined man to be good to his parents. But if they urge you to ascribe to Me as partner that of which you have no knowledge, then do not obey them. To Me will be your return, whereat I will inform you concerning what you used to do. (8)

Messages

1- Kindness to parents is a human matter, not merely a matter of faith. «و وصّینا الانسان»

2- Benevolence and kindness to parents are unconditional. «حُسناً» (It has no racial, age, regional, educational, social, political, economic, or faith-based condition; one must show benevolence even if they are disbelievers or polytheists.)

3- Deviant parents strive to deviate their children. «جاهداك»

4- We should not misuse the respect of others. (Parents should not invite their children to polytheism merely because their children show them respect.) «و اِن جاهداك»

5- Children must have the power to choose the correct ideology. «جاهداك لتشرك بى... فلا تطعهما»

6- Polytheism lacks scientific proof or argument. «ما لیس لك به علم»

7- In the matter of Monotheism and polytheism, we must not compromise or reconcile with anyone. «فلا تطعهما»

8- Benevolence to parents is absolute and perpetual; however, obedience to parents is conditional upon them not distancing a person from Allah. «فلا تطعهما»

9- Faith in the Resurrection is the guarantee for the implementation of Divine injunctions. «وصّینا... الّى مرجعكم»

10- Allah is aware of all human deeds. «فاُنبّئكم بما كنتم تعملون»

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ لَنُدْخِلَنَّهُمْ فِى ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ (۹)
Those who have faith and do righteous deeds, We will surely admit them among the righteous. (9)

Key Points

The meaning of "the believers" (مؤمنان) in this verse, considering the previous verse, refers to children who, while under the authority of their parents, are invited to polytheism. If they resist and are forced to leave their home and region to preserve their faith, even though separation from their parents is difficult for them, Allah will, in return, admit them among the righteous on the Day of Resurrection. (110) It was stated in the two preceding verses that the reward for faith and righteous deeds is the covering of sins and the receiving of a good recompense. In this verse, He says: those who possess faith and righteous deeds will also be placed among the righteous individuals. In another verse, we read: joining the righteous is among the supplications of Prophet Abraham and Prophet Joseph. «الحقنى بالصّالحین» (111)

110) Tafsir al-Mizan. 111) Yusuf, 101 and Shu'ara, 83.

Messages

1- Faith is inseparable from action. «آمنوا و عملوا»

2- Mere good work does not place a person among the righteous. Good work that arises from faith in Allah is valuable. «آمنوا... لندخلنّهم فى الصالحین»

Ayah

وَمِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ مَن يَقُولُ ءَامَنَّا بِٱللَّهِ فَإِذَآ أُوذِىَ فِى ٱللَّهِ جَعَلَ فِتْنَةَ ٱلنَّاسِ كَعَذَابِ ٱللَّهِ وَلَئِن جَآءَ نَصْرٌ مِّن رَّبِّكَ لَيَقُولُنَّ إِنَّا كُنَّا مَعَكُمْ ۚ أَوَلَيْسَ ٱللَّهُ بِأَعْلَمَ بِمَا فِى صُدُورِ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۱٠)
Among the people there are those who say, ‘We have faith in Allah,’ but if such a one is tormented in Allah’s cause, he takes persecution by the people for Allah’s punishment. Yet if there comes any help from your Lord, they will say, ‘We were indeed with you.’ Does not Allah know best what is in the breasts of the creatures? (10)

Key Points

Apparently, the meaning of "العالمین" (Al-Alamin/the worlds) is every conscious being, including Jinn, angels, and humans. (112) In the first Ayah of this Surah, we read that you should not assume that We accept your declaration of faith without trial. "أحسب النّاس أن یتركوا ..." This Ayah describes one example of that trial.

112) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Messages

1- The declaration of faith by some people is verbal, not heartfelt. "و مِن الناس مَن یقول آمنّا"

2- Sometimes faith leads to persecution and harm from people, which must be endured. "آمنّا - اوذى فى اللَّه"

3- The believer is resilient. "ولنصبرنّ على ما اذیتمونا" (113) But the hypocrite is unstable. "فاذا اوذى ... جعل..."

4- True faith becomes evident during hardships. "فاذا اوذى فى اللَّه" Imam Ali عليه السلام says: "فى تقلّب الاحوال علم جواهر الرّجال" (114) The essence of men is revealed during the ups and downs and various circumstances.

5- The hypocrite is an opportunist; at the time of victory, he considers himself a believer and insists on receiving benefits. "لیقولنّ انّا كنّا معكم"

6- Hypocrisy and concealment are useless; Allah is aware of what is within everyone. "أولیس اللَّه باعلم بما فى صدور العالمین"

113) Ibrahim, 12.

114) Nahj al-Balaghah, Maxim 217.

Ayah

وَلَيَعْلَمَنَّ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَلَيَعْلَمَنَّ ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقِينَ (۱۱)
Allah shall surely ascertain those who have faith, and He shall surely ascertain the hypocrites. (11)

Key Points

The previous Ayah mentioned that the hypocrites emphatically expressed their alignment with the believers and the general populace, [saying,] «انّا كنّا معكم» (We were with you). In this Ayah, Allah also states emphatically: I know everyone. [This is indicated by] «ولیعلمنّ اللّه».

Although Allah is aware of the inner thoughts of everyone and knows the believer from the hypocrite, He distinguishes them from one another through trial and tribulation (fitnah and test). (115) (115) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Belief in Divine knowledge is a factor in abandoning hypocrisy. «و لیعلمنّ المنافقین»

2- Only Allah knows the true and false believers. «و لیعلمنّ اللّه الّذین آمنوا و لیعلمنّ المنافقین»

Ayah

وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لِلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱتَّبِعُوا۟ سَبِيلَنَا وَلْنَحْمِلْ خَطَـٰيَـٰكُمْ وَمَا هُم بِحَـٰمِلِينَ مِنْ خَطَـٰيَـٰهُم مِّن شَىْءٍ ۖ إِنَّهُمْ لَكَـٰذِبُونَ (۱۲)
The faithless say to the faithful, ‘Follow our way and we will bear [responsibility for] your iniquities.’ They will not bear anything of their iniquities. They are indeed liars. (12)

Key Points

Deviants, when inviting and encouraging others to commit wrongdoings, say: If this act has a sin, it is upon us. While no one can take responsibility for the sin of another. «و لْنَحمل خطایاكم و ما هم بحاملین» Perhaps it can be said that the disbelievers, who desire people to abandon their faith. «وَدَّ كثیرٌ من اهل الكتاب لو یَرُدّونَكم من بعد ایمانكم كُفّارا»(116), carry out their work in several stages: A: By harming the believers, they prevent them from believing. «یصدّون عن سبیل اللَّه»(117) B: They wage war. «لا یزالون یقاتلونكم حتّى یردّوكم عن دینكم اِن استطاعوا»(118) C: They say: At least follow our path in action. «اتّبعوا سبیلنا» If you do so, you will no longer be harmed or tormented, and we will take full responsibility for your apostasy. «و لْنَحمل خطایاكم» D: If they do not succeed in any of the above actions, they wish that you would at least compromise and appease them. «ودّوا لو تدهن فیدهنون»(119)

116) Baqarah, 109 . 117) Hajj, 25 and A'raf, 45 . 118) Baqarah, 217 . 119) Qalam, 9.

Messages

1- The enemies will not give up on you and will propagate their goals. (Muslims were afflicted both by the physical torture and harm inflicted by the disbelievers, «فتنة الناس», and by their cultural, propaganda, and psychological attacks.) «و قال الّذین كفروا للّذین آمنوا اتّبعوا سبیلنا»

2- Apostasy and returning to disbelief is a great sin that the disbelievers claim they will compensate for. «و لْنَحمل خطایاكم»

3- In the Islamic worldview, no soul carries the burden of another. «و لا تزر وازرة وزر اُخرى»(120) But this is not the case in the thinking of others. «و لْنَحمل خطایاكم» (The Church still buys the sins of others!)

4- The promises of the disbelievers and Satan are hollow and baseless. «و ماهم بحاملین» (Satan also commands man to disbelieve, but after he disbelieves, he abandons him. «اذ قال للانسان اكفر فلمّا كفر قال انّى برىٌ منك»(121))

120) An'am, 164 . 121) Hashr, 16 .

Ayah

وَلَيَحْمِلُنَّ أَثْقَالَهُمْ وَأَثْقَالًا مَّعَ أَثْقَالِهِمْ ۖ وَلَيُسْـَٔلُنَّ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ عَمَّا كَانُوا۟ يَفْتَرُونَ (۱۳)
But they will carry their own burdens and other burdens along with their own burdens, and they will surely be questioned on the Day of Resurrection concerning that which they used to fabricate. (13)

Key Points

| # Question: How did the previous Ayah state that no soul shall bear the burden of another's sin, yet this Ayah states that those who mislead others shall bear the burden of others' sins?! # Answer: The first Ayah was in response to the disbelievers who told the Muslims: "Follow us, and your sins will be upon us, and you will bear no responsibility." Allah stated: "This is not the case, and everyone is responsible for his own sins." However, this Ayah states that the person who was deceived is responsible for his own action; but the deceiver, in addition to the responsibility for his own action, is also responsible for the sins of those he deceived, without anything being reduced from the sin of the deceived person. In the Tafsirs Durr al-Manthur and Namūnah, numerous narrations state that whoever opens a path for others in a good or bad deed, in addition to the reward or punishment given to each of those who perform and follow that path, a reward or punishment is also given to the guide, founder, and initiator of that path.

Messages

1- Those who cause deviation also bear the burden of those who deviated. «اثقالاً مع اثقالهم»

2- Misleading others places a heavy burden upon a person. «اثقالا»

3- The burden of sin is heavy. «اثقالهم»

4- Fabricating lies against Allah and the Prophet is the method of the disbelievers. «كانوا یفترون»

Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا نُوحًا إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِۦ فَلَبِثَ فِيهِمْ أَلْفَ سَنَةٍ إِلَّا خَمْسِينَ عَامًا فَأَخَذَهُمُ ٱلطُّوفَانُ وَهُمْ ظَـٰلِمُونَ (۱٤)
Certainly We sent Noah to his people, and he remained with them for a thousand-less-fifty years. Then the flood overtook them while they were wrongdoers. (14)
فَأَنجَيْنَـٰهُ وَأَصْحَـٰبَ ٱلسَّفِينَةِ وَجَعَلْنَـٰهَآ ءَايَةً لِّلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۱٥)
Then We delivered him and those who were in the Ark, and made it a sign for all the nations. (15)

Key Points

At the beginning of the Surah, we read that the Exalted God (Allah) said: people and those who claim faith are not left unchecked; We will test everyone. From this Ayah onward, we read the stories of Noah, Abraham, Lot, Shu'ayb, Hud, Salih, and Moses (علیهم السلام), and become familiar with examples of the trials faced by previous nations. The only Prophet whose period of prophethood is stated in the Quran is Noah. Nine hundred and fifty years is the duration of Noah's prophethood until the time of the flood; however, the duration of his life and his prophethood after the flood is not mentioned.

Messages

1- In the method of propagation (Da'wah), it is appropriate to state the general principles first, and then elaborate on the details and examples. In the previous Ayat, we read: «و لقد فتنّاالّذین من قبلهم» (And We certainly tried those before them), and now He explains in detail: «و لقد ارسلنا نوحاً» (And We certainly sent Noah).

2- Familiarity with the history of past nations is a focus of the Quran. «و لقد ارسلنا نوحاً» (The narration of the history of previous Prophets is a source of comfort for the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله.)

3- The Prophets were among the people. «فیهم» (If a Prophet abandons the people, he is punished. As we read concerning Jonah: «فالتقمه الحوت»(122) [So the whale swallowed him]—after distancing himself from the people, a whale swallowed him.)

4- By mentioning the long lifespan of man, the Quran does not consider it impossible. «الف سنة ...»

5- If the capacity and readiness for acceptance are absent, even a thousand years of propagation will have no effect. «الف سنة الاّ خمسین عاما»

6- Patience and steadfastness are necessary in education and propagation. «الف سنة الاّخمسین عاماً» This Ayah demonstrates the steadfastness of Noah (عليه السلام) and the obstinacy of the people.

7- We should report statistics accurately. «الف سنة الاّ خمسین عاماً»

8- Disregard for the message of the Prophets is oppression (zulm), and oppression is the key to Divine wrath. «فاخذهم الطّوفان و هم ظالمون»

9- The Prophets and their followers are safe from Divine wrath. «فانجیناه واصحاب السفینة»

10- Noah's long life, the building of the Ark, and the submersion of the entire earth were by the command of Allah. «ارسلنا... الف سنة... فانجیناه و اصحاب السّفینة»

11- Divine help does not mean abandoning effort and striving. Noah and his companions built the Ark. «السّفینة»

12- Some individuals and events transcend time and place. «آیة للعالمین» (122) Saffat, 142.

Ayah

وَإِبْرَٰهِيمَ إِذْ قَالَ لِقَوْمِهِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَٱتَّقُوهُ ۖ ذَٰلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (۱٦)
And Abraham, when he said to his people, ‘Worship Allah and be wary of Him. That is better for you, should you know. (16)

Key Points

Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham) عليه السلام is the second Prophet of Resolve (Ulul-'Azm), who was sent after Nuh (Noah) عليه السلام.

Messages

1- Monotheism (Tawhid) and piety (Taqwa) are the priorities of the Prophets' mission. «اعبدوا اللّه واتقّوه»

2- Worship without piety is ineffective. «اعبدوا اللّه واتقّوه»

3- Attaining goodness depends on worshipping Allah and having piety. «اعبدوا اللّه واتقّوه ذلكم خیر»

4- Those who turn to things other than Allah and corruption instead of monotheism and piety are truly ignorant. (A scholar without piety is ignorant.) «ذلكم خیرٌ لكم ان كنتم تعلمون»

5- Through knowledge and its advancement, one can grasp the benefits of devotion (ta'abbud) and piety (taqwa). «ذلكم خیرٌ لكم ان كنتم تعلمون»

Ayah

إِنَّمَا تَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ أَوْثَـٰنًا وَتَخْلُقُونَ إِفْكًا ۚ إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ تَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ لَا يَمْلِكُونَ لَكُمْ رِزْقًا فَٱبْتَغُوا۟ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرِّزْقَ وَٱعْبُدُوهُ وَٱشْكُرُوا۟ لَهُۥٓ ۖ إِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ (۱۷)
What you worship instead of Allah are mere idols, and you invent a lie. Indeed those whom you worship besides Allah have no control over your provision. So seek all [your] provision from Allah, and worship Him and thank Him, and to Him you shall be brought back.’ (17)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, Prophet Ibrahim invited the people to the worship and obedience of Allah, and in this Ayah, He rejects the worship of anything other than Allah as futile and fruitless, and at the end of the Ayah, He emphasizes the worship of Allah once again.

Polytheism (Shirk) and idol worship have no proof or logic, because: Firstly: Idols are nothing more than inanimate objects. «أَوْثَانًا» Secondly: The idolaters themselves are their creators. «تَخْلُقُونَ» Thirdly: They possess no benefit whatsoever for the polytheists and idolaters. «لَا يَمْلِكُونَ لَكُمْ»

In this Ayah, Prophet Ibrahim addressed the polytheists three times, and this is a sign of direct dialogue with the misguided. «تَعْبُدُونَ - تَخْلُقُونَ - تَعْبُدُونَ»

Messages

1- Those who follow a crooked path justify their actions. «تَخْلُقُونَ إِفْكًا»

2- One of the motivations for worshipping other than Allah is the desire for sustenance and prosperity. «لَا يَمْلِكُونَ لَكُمْ رِزْقًا»

3- Things other than Allah do not possess the ability to provide sustenance; it is not that they can provide but choose not to. «لَا يَمْلِكُونَ» (He did not say: «لَا يَرْزُقُونَكُمْ»)

4- In guidance (Irshad) and propagation (Tabligh), after proving the falsehood of an idea, show the path of truth. «لَا يَمْلِكُونَ لَكُمْ رِزْقًا فَابْتَغُوا عِنْدَ اللَّهِ الرِّزْقَ»

5- Unless people despair of things other than Allah, they will not turn to Allah. «لَا يَمْلِكُونَ ... فَابْتَغُوا عِنْدَ اللَّهِ»

6- Allah is the Provider (Raziq), but man must also strive. «فَابْتَغُوا»

7- First, material and welfare needs must be addressed, and then people should be guided. «فَابْتَغُوا عِنْدَ اللَّهِ الرِّزْقَ وَاعْبُدُوهُ»

8- The source of sustenance is with Allah. «عِنْدَ اللَّهِ الرِّزْقَ»

9- Worship is only worthy of Him in whose hand is the power. «عِنْدَ اللَّهِ الرِّزْقَ وَاعْبُدُوهُ»

10- The guarantee for the implementation of divine commands is the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah). «إِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ»

Ayahs

وَإِن تُكَذِّبُوا۟ فَقَدْ كَذَّبَ أُمَمٌ مِّن قَبْلِكُمْ ۖ وَمَا عَلَى ٱلرَّسُولِ إِلَّا ٱلْبَلَـٰغُ ٱلْمُبِينُ (۱۸)
If you impugn [the Apostle’s teaching], then [other] nations have impugned [likewise] before you, and the Apostle’s duty is only to communicate in clear terms. (18)
أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ كَيْفَ يُبْدِئُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْخَلْقَ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُۥٓ ۚ إِنَّ ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ يَسِيرٌ (۱۹)
Have they not regarded how Allah originates the creation? Then He will bring it back. That is indeed easy for Allah. (19)

Key Points

These two verses refer to three principles common among all religions: A: Monotheism (Tawhid). «یُبدى ء اللَّهُ الخلق» B: Prophethood (Nubuwwah). «و ما على الرّسول الاّالبلاغ» C: Resurrection (Ma'ad). «ثمّ یعیده»

Messages

1- All Prophets have had opponents. Therefore, we should not fear the opposition of enemies. «كذّب اممٌ ...»

2- Historical events are similar and repeatable. «اُممٌ من قبلك»

3- Familiarity with history provides a form of solace for humanity. «كذّب اُممٌ من قبلك»

4- The denial by opponents should not prevent propagation (of the message). «و ما على الرسول ...»

5- People are free to choose their beliefs, and the Prophet cannot compel them. «و ان تكذبوا... ما على الرّسول الاّالبلاغ المبین»

I have told you what is required for the conveyance (of the message); You may choose to take counsel from my words, or choose distress.

6- Guidance (Irshad) and propagation (Tabligh) must be clear and explicit. «البلاغ المبین»

7- By posing questions, we should awaken the innate nature (Fitrah) of the heedless. «أولم یروا...»

8- Islam is a religion of thought and invites all people to reflect. «أولم یروا...»

9- The universe is a manifestation of Allah’s power in creating the life and death of phenomena. «یبدى ء اللّه الخلق ثمّ یعیده»

10- Creation is temporal and transient, and ultimately they return to Allah. «یبدى ء اللّه الخلق ثمّ یعیده»

11- Belief in the Originator (Mabda') leads to belief in the Resurrection (Ma'ad), while ignorance of Allah’s power leads to the denial of the Resurrection. «یبدى ء اللّه الخلق ثمّ یعیده»

12- Creating, causing death, and resurrecting are the continuous actions of Allah. «یبدى ء اللّه الخلق ثمّ یعیده» (The imperfect verb [Mudari'] indicates continuity and permanence.)

13- The initial creation and the recreation on the Day of Judgment are easy for Allah; they require only a single Will. «انّ ذلك على اللّه یسیر»

Ayah

قُلْ سِيرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَٱنظُرُوا۟ كَيْفَ بَدَأَ ٱلْخَلْقَ ۚ ثُمَّ ٱللَّهُ يُنشِئُ ٱلنَّشْأَةَ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةَ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (۲٠)
Say, ‘Travel over the land and observe how He has originated the creation.’ Then Allah will bring about the genesis of the Hereafter. Indeed Allah has power over all things. (20)

Messages

1- Tourism, journeying, studying nature, and purposeful excursions are a duty and a value. «قل سیروا فى الارض فانظروا»

2- Traveling in nature can lead man to contemplation. «قل سیروا... فانظروا»

3- By precise study of creation, we can remove our doubt and skepticism and know Allah better. «فانظروا...»

4- The best proof of the Resurrection is Allah's display of power in bringing creatures into existence in the first instance. «كیف بدء الخلق ثمّ اللّه یُنشى ءُ النّشأة الآخرة»

Ayahs

يُعَذِّبُ مَن يَشَآءُ وَيَرْحَمُ مَن يَشَآءُ ۖ وَإِلَيْهِ تُقْلَبُونَ (۲۱)
He will punish whomever He wishes and have mercy on whomever He wishes, and to Him you will be returned. (21)
وَمَآ أَنتُم بِمُعْجِزِينَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ ۖ وَمَا لَكُم مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مِن وَلِىٍّ وَلَا نَصِيرٍ (۲۲)
You cannot frustrate Him on the earth or in the sky, nor do you have besides Allah any guardian or helper. (22)

Key Points

The educational method of the Quran is such that whenever it mentions Allah's love (mercy) and wrath, it first speaks of love and mercy, and then of wrath and punishment. However, since this Ayah follows the denial of the disbelievers (kuffar), it first speaks about punishment and then about mercy. In the Noble Quran, wherever the phrase "مَن یشاء" (whom He wills) and talk of Allah's will and desire appears, the divine will (mashiyyah) is based on wisdom and justice, because the Divine Will is not without wisdom. We read in Majma' al-Bayan: A "ولىّ" (Wali/Guardian) is someone who helps without being asked, and a "نصیر" (Nasir/Helper) is someone who aids a person upon their request, and the disbelievers are deprived of both.

Messages

1- The enemies of religion, despite all planning, effort, and conspiracy, cannot overcome the Divine Will or escape the sphere of His wrath. «و ما انتم بمعجزین»

2- To eliminate polytheism (shirk), all types of illusions and false beliefs must be negated. «ما انتم - و لا فى السماء - و لانصیر» He tells the disbelievers and enemies of religion: Neither are you effective on your own, «ما انتم بمعجزین», nor can others accomplish anything. «ما لكم... من ولىّ ولانصیر»

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ وَلِقَآئِهِۦٓ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ يَئِسُوا۟ مِن رَّحْمَتِى وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (۲۳)
Those who deny the signs of Allah and the encounter with Him—they have despaired of My mercy, and for such there is a painful punishment. (23)

Messages

1- The only group that is despairing and deprived of the expansive Divine Mercy are the disbelievers (Kuffar). «والّذین كفروا... اولئك یئسُوا» And in Ayah 87 of Surah Yusuf, we read: «انّه لا ییئسُ من روح اللَّه الاّ القوم الكافرون»

Ayah

فَمَا كَانَ جَوَابَ قَوْمِهِۦٓ إِلَّآ أَن قَالُوا۟ ٱقْتُلُوهُ أَوْ حَرِّقُوهُ فَأَنجَىٰهُ ٱللَّهُ مِنَ ٱلنَّارِ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ (۲٤)
But the only answer of his people was that they said, ‘Kill him, or burn him.’ Then Allah delivered him from the fire. There are indeed signs in that for a people who have faith. (24)

Key Points

The disbelievers and the Nimrodians, in order to combat the true Abrahamic religion, decided to kill him. Thus, by preparing a vast fire and throwing Prophet Abraham into it, they carried out their intention; but Allah saved the Prophet by turning that fire into a garden. «فأنجاه اللّه من النار»

Messages

1- Disbelievers lack logic; their logic is execution and torture. «اُقتلوهُ اوحرّقوه»

2- The disbelievers may differ in method, but they do not differ in goal. «اُقتلوهُ اوحرّقوه»

3- Prophets are under Divine protection. «فأنجاه اللّه من النار»

4- The Will of Allah prevails over all wills, tools, means, and laws of the universe. «اُقتلوه او حَرّقوه فأنجاه اللَّه من النار»

5- The victory of one believer over all the disbelievers, the neutralization of the most severe conspiracies, and the turning of the fire into a garden—each is an Ayah (sign) among the Divine signs. «انّ فى ذلك لآیاتٍ»

6- Learning lessons from history requires a sound nature and a pure soul. «لآیات لقوم یؤمنون» Yes, faith is influential in human insight and clear-sightedness.

Ayah

وَقَالَ إِنَّمَا ٱتَّخَذْتُم مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ أَوْثَـٰنًا مَّوَدَّةَ بَيْنِكُمْ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا ۖ ثُمَّ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ يَكْفُرُ بَعْضُكُم بِبَعْضٍ وَيَلْعَنُ بَعْضُكُم بَعْضًا وَمَأْوَىٰكُمُ ٱلنَّارُ وَمَا لَكُم مِّن نَّـٰصِرِينَ (۲٥)
He said, ‘You have taken idols [for worship] besides Allah for the sake of [mutual] affection amongst yourselves in the life of the world. Then on the Day of Resurrection you will disown one another and curse one another, and the Fire will be your abode, and you will not have any helpers.’ (25)

Key Points

Among the idol-worshipping tribes, idols were central, and every tribe had a specific idol. For example: Quraysh had an idol named 'Uzzā, the Thaqīf tribe had the idol Lāt, and Aws and Khazraj had Manāt. These idols were the means by which the idolaters connected with their ancestors.

We read in Ayah 17 that Ibrāhīm (Abraham) عليه السلام, before being threatened with fire, told His people: «انّما تعبدون من دون اللّه اوثاناً» (You are only worshipping idols instead of Allah). After being saved from the fire, He stated with greater decisiveness and clarity: «انّما اتّخذتم من دون اللّه اوثاناً». Therefore, throwing Ibrāhīm عليه السلام into the fire had no effect on His preaching and goal, and He did not abandon the people.

Imam Sādiq عليه السلام told one of His companions: No group follows a leader except that on the Day of Judgment, curses will be exchanged between that leader and his followers, except for you who are in the line of the Ahl al-Bayt of the Prophet عليهم السلام. «انّه لیس من قوم ائتمّوا بامام فى الدنیا الاّ جاء یوم القیامة یلعنهم و یلعنونه الاّ انتم...»(123)

Sometimes, the plans and actions of the enemy must be answered more crushingly; in the previous Ayah, we read that the disbelievers planned to throw Ibrāhīm into the fire, «حرّقوه» (Burn him!), and Allah answers them: Your eternal abode is the Fire. «مأواكم النار». Indeed, sometimes the reward for throwing a clod of earth is a stone.

123) Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn; Kāfī, Vol. 8, p. 146.

Messages

  1. Emotional bonds have a great influence on human beliefs and doctrines. «انما اتّخذتم... اوثانا مودّة بینكم»
  2. Non-Divine friendships are seasonal and temporary, and will eventually turn into enmity and cursing. «مودّة بینكم فى الحیاة الدنیا... یلعن بعضكم بعضا»
  3. We must be forward-looking in choosing our path. «مودّة بینكم... مأواكم النار»
  4. Non-Divine friendships will have no utility whatsoever on the Day of Judgment. «ما لكم من ناصرین»

Ayah

۞ فَـَٔامَنَ لَهُۥ لُوطٌ ۘ وَقَالَ إِنِّى مُهَاجِرٌ إِلَىٰ رَبِّىٓ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (۲٦)
Thereupon Lot believed in him, and he said, ‘Indeed I am migrating toward my Lord. Indeed He is the All-mighty, the All-wise.’ (26)

Key Points

Most commentators have said that the meaning of "the emigrant" (al-muhājir) is Prophet Abraham, just as the Prophet said in another Ayah: «انّى ذاهب الى ربّى سیهدین»(124). And some have said that the meaning is Prophet Lot.

124) Saffat, 99.

Messages

1- Prophets believe in one another. «فآمن له لوط» (Lot believed in him). Of course, this also indicates the loneliness and isolation of Prophet Abraham.

2- Sometimes there were several Prophets at the same time, but the leadership (imāmah) and guidance were the responsibility of only one of them. «فآمن له لوط»

3- The faith of Prophet Lot was a form of declaring support and strengthening Prophet Abraham. «فآمن له» (He believed for him/in his cause) was used, not «آمن به» (He believed in him).

4- Goal-oriented migration (Hijrah) is a means of growth and spiritual training. «الى ربّى» (To my Lord).

5- Men of God are not dependent on land, time, or any individual or group. «اِنّى مهاجر الى ربّى» (Indeed, I am emigrating to my Lord).

6- We should strengthen the spirit of migration within ourselves by remembering Divine favors. «مهاجر الى ربّى انّه هو العزیز الحكیم» (I am emigrating to my Lord. Indeed, He is the Almighty, the Wise.) (Connection to the Almighty God compensates for feelings of isolation.)

Ayah

وَوَهَبْنَا لَهُۥٓ إِسْحَـٰقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ وَجَعَلْنَا فِى ذُرِّيَّتِهِ ٱلنُّبُوَّةَ وَٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَءَاتَيْنَـٰهُ أَجْرَهُۥ فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا ۖ وَإِنَّهُۥ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ لَمِنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ (۲۷)
And We gave him Isaac and Jacob, and We ordained among his descendants prophethood and the Book, and We gave him his reward in this world, and in the Hereafter he will indeed be among the Righteous. (27)

Key Points

The word 'ajr (أجر) is used for both worldly and otherworldly rewards, but the word 'ujrat (اُجرت) is used only for worldly rewards. (125) The reward for all that sincerity, effort, and migration of Prophet Ibrahim عليه السلام consists of four gifts mentioned in this Ayah: First: Pure children. Second: The station of prophethood in his lineage and progeny. Third: A good name in this world. Fourth: Honor in the Hereafter.

Explanation: Ya'qub, Yusuf, Harun, Musa, Sulayman, Zakariyya, Yahya, and 'Isa علیهم السلام were all descendants of Prophet Ishaq, and the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله is from the progeny of Isma'il; therefore, all the Prophets of Firm Resolve (Ulul-'Azm) after Prophet Ibrahim were from his lineage. Salvation from the fire of the Nimrudians, the attraction of hearts towards Mecca, the renewal of Ibrahim's memories during the Hajj rituals, rising from obscurity (126) to become the Shaykh al-Anbiya (Chief of the Prophets), and having pure progeny are all examples of the Divine favors granted to Ibrahim عليه السلام in this world. "و آتیناه اجره فى الدنیا" (And We gave him his reward in the world.)

Being righteous and being counted among the righteous is a status that the Prophets wished for: Yusuf عليه السلام asked Allah: "و ألحقنى بالصالحین" (and join me with the righteous) (127). Sulayman عليه السلام prayed: "و أدخلنى برحمتك فى عبادك الصالحین" (and admit me by Your mercy among Your righteous servants) (128). Shu'ayb عليه السلام told Musa عليه السلام: "ستجدنى ان شاءاللَّه من الصالحین" (You will find me, if Allah wills, among the righteous) (129). Ibrahim عليه السلام requested of Allah: "و ألحقنى بالصّالحین" (and join me with the righteous) (130). And here, Allah says about Prophet Ibrahim: "و اِنّه فى الآخرة لَمِن الصالحین" (And indeed, in the Hereafter, he will surely be among the righteous.)

125) Tafsir al-Mizan. 126) فتىً یذكرهم یقال له ابراهیم» Anbiya, 60. 127) Yusuf, 101. 128) Naml, 19. 129) Qasas, 27. 130) Shu'ara, 83.

Messages

  1. A righteous child is a Divine gift. "و وهبنا له" (And We bestowed upon him)

  2. We should announce the reward for working for Allah to the people so that it becomes an incentive for others. "وهبنا له" (We bestowed upon him)

  3. The foundation for receiving Divine favors is sincerity. "مهاجر الى ربّى" (migrating to my Lord) - "وهبنا... جعلنا..." (We bestowed... We made...)

  4. A person's grandchild, like a child, is a Divine gift. "اسحاق و یعقوب" (Ishaq and Ya'qub) (Considering that Ya'qub is the son of Ishaq) "و من وراء اسحاق یعقوب" (and after Ishaq, Ya'qub) (131)

  5. The reward for the sincere services and good deeds of the father is reflected in his progeny. "جعلنا فى ذرّیته" (We placed in his progeny) Of course, the worthiness of the progeny is also a condition. "قال و من ذرّیّتى قال لاینال عهدى الظالمین" (He said, "And from my progeny?" [Allah] said, "My covenant does not include the wrongdoers.") (132)

  6. The reward for those who migrate is not limited. "مهاجر الى ربّى" (migrating to my Lord) - "وهبنا... جعلنا... آتینا..." (We bestowed... We made... We gave...)

  7. No Prophet had as numerous a progeny and lineage as Prophet Ibrahim. "و جعلنا فى ذرّیته ..." (And We placed in his progeny...)

  8. Religious leaders must be appointed by Allah. "جعلنا... النبوّة و الكتاب" (We made... the Prophethood and the Book)

  9. This world and the Hereafter can be combined. "آتیناه أجره فى الدّنیا و انّه فى الآخرة لمن الصّالحین" (We gave him his reward in the world, and indeed, in the Hereafter, he will surely be among the righteous.)

131) Hud, 71. 132) Baqarah, 124.

Ayah

وَلُوطًا إِذْ قَالَ لِقَوْمِهِۦٓ إِنَّكُمْ لَتَأْتُونَ ٱلْفَـٰحِشَةَ مَا سَبَقَكُم بِهَا مِنْ أَحَدٍ مِّنَ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۲۸)
And Lot, when he said to his people, ‘You indeed commit an indecency none in the world has ever committed before you! (28)

Key Points

The word fāḥishah means an act or a word whose ugliness is manifest and clear. Since the vile act of sodomy and homosexuality was prevalent among the people of Prophet Lot, the Quran refers to it as fāḥishah.

Messages

1- Religious leaders must pay attention to the corruption, anomalies, and public dangers of society and strive to eliminate them. (Prophet Lot warned concerning the notorious sin of his time.) «قال ... لتأتون الفاحشة»

2- Not every innovation is valuable. (Innovating sin is an anti-value.) «ما سبقكم بها»

Ayahs

أَئِنَّكُمْ لَتَأْتُونَ ٱلرِّجَالَ وَتَقْطَعُونَ ٱلسَّبِيلَ وَتَأْتُونَ فِى نَادِيكُمُ ٱلْمُنكَرَ ۖ فَمَا كَانَ جَوَابَ قَوْمِهِۦٓ إِلَّآ أَن قَالُوا۟ ٱئْتِنَا بِعَذَابِ ٱللَّهِ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ (۲۹)
What! Do you come to men, and cut off the way, and commit outrages in your gatherings?’ But the only answer of his people was that they said, ‘Bring down us Allah’s punishment, if you are truthful.’ (29)
قَالَ رَبِّ ٱنصُرْنِى عَلَى ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلْمُفْسِدِينَ (۳٠)
He said, ‘My Lord! Help me against this corruptive lot.’ (30)

Key Points

The word nādī means a public assembly (or council). We read in the commentaries (tafāsīr) that the people of Lot had completely fallen in terms of manners and morals, and were drowned in corruption and depravity; to the extent that in public assemblies, they would speak obscenities to one another, gamble, strip naked in front of the crowd, throw stones at passersby, pass gas publicly, commit sodomy with travelers, and seize their possessions. The request for punishment has also been expressed by the nations of other prophets; the people of Noah and 'Ad said: «فائتنا بما تعدنا»(133), the people of Thamud asked Prophet Salih: «فاتنا بما تعدنا»(134), and similarly, during the time of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله, a group said: «ائتنا بعذاب»(135). It is possible that the meaning of qaṭ' sabīl (cutting the way) in the Ayah refers to blocking the road for people in order to plunder their wealth or commit sodomy with them.

133) Hud, 32. 134) Al-A'raf, 70. 135) Al-Anfal, 32.

Messages

1- Despite the natural and lawful way of marriage to satisfy sexual instinct, sodomy and homosexuality are astonishing. «أَئِنَّكُمْ لَتَأْتُونَ الرِّجَالَ»

2- The prevalence of corruption does not prevent Nahy 'anil-Munkar (forbidding evil). «أَئِنَّكُمْ لَتَأْتُونَ الرِّجَالَ»

3- Moral corruption leads a person to ideological corruption. (Drowning in sin and ignoring moral values caused doubt in the prophethood of Prophet Lot.) «لَتَأْتُونَ الرِّجَالَ - ائْتِنَا بِعَذَابِ اللَّهِ إِنْ كُنْتَ مِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ»

4- Allah has provided a way for the satisfaction of the sexual instinct and has not left man in a dead end. «السَّبِيلَ»

5- Public sin is among the ugliest of sins. «فِي نَادِيكُمُ الْمُنْكَرَ»

6- Public security and chastity must be respected and preserved. «فِي نَادِيكُمُ الْمُنْكَرَ»

7- The consequence of public sin is eternal disgrace. (The people of Lot committed public sin for a short time, but they became infamous for dozens of centuries.) «فِي نَادِيكُمُ الْمُنْكَرَ...»

8- Sincerity and truthfulness are considered good, even in the view of the misguided. «إِنْ كُنْتَ مِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ»

9- Sodomy is munkar (evil) and fāḥishah (an abomination), «الْمُنْكَرَ - لَتَأْتُونَ الْفَاحِشَةَ», and the sodomite is a corrupter upon the earth (mufsid fī al-arḍ). «رَبِّ انْصُرْنِي عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْمُفْسِدِينَ»

10- Being alone is not an obstacle to forbidding evil (Nahy 'anil-Munkar). Although Prophet Lot was alone, he stood against deviation and the deviants and sought help from Allah. «رَبِّ انْصُرْنِي»

11- We must always strive to overcome the corrupters, rather than accepting their rule. «رَبِّ انْصُرْنِي عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْمُفْسِدِينَ»

Ayah

وَلَمَّا جَآءَتْ رُسُلُنَآ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ بِٱلْبُشْرَىٰ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا مُهْلِكُوٓا۟ أَهْلِ هَـٰذِهِ ٱلْقَرْيَةِ ۖ إِنَّ أَهْلَهَا كَانُوا۟ ظَـٰلِمِينَ (۳۱)
And when Our messengers came to Abraham with the good news, they said, ‘We are indeed going to destroy the people of this town. Its people are indeed wrongdoers.’ (31)

Key Points

The supplication (du'a) of Prophet Lot (عليه السلام) for victory over the corrupt people was answered, and Allah sent down the angels of punishment upon them. The region of the people of Lot was named "Sodom" and was near the region of Prophet Abraham (عليه السلام). Prophet Abraham (عليه السلام) and his wife Sarah were about one hundred years old when Allah gave them the glad tidings of the birth of Isaac. (136) Imam Sajjad (عليه السلام), in the Sahifah al-Sajjadiyyah, names groups of angels, each having a specific responsibility. However, the angels who visited Prophet Abraham (عليه السلام) had several objectives: they were commissioned to give the glad tidings of a son to Prophet Abraham (عليه السلام), commissioned to announce the punishment of the people of Lot, and also the executors of the Divine punishment.

136) Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Hierarchy must be observed. (The angels commissioned to destroy the people of Lot had to first inform Prophet Abraham (عليه السلام), who was a Prophet of firm resolve (ulu al-'azm) and possessed a Divine Law, and then inform Prophet Lot (عليه السلام), who was the preacher of Abraham's religion.) «جائت رسلنا ابراهیم»

2- The supplication (du'a) of Allah's righteous servants is answered. «ربّ انصرنى ... انّا مهلكوا»

3- Angels are the agents of Divine wrath and Divine mercy. «جائت رسلنا... بالبشرى... انّا مهلكوا»

4- The sin of sodomy (liwat) was committed by a lustful and deviant group. However, when Allah's wrath descended, all the inhabitants of the region were destroyed, perhaps because due to their inappropriate silence, they were all labeled as oppressors (zalimin). «انّ اهلها كانوا ظالمین»

5- Oppression (zulm) is the cause of destruction. «انّا مهلكوا... ظالمین»

Ayah

قَالَ إِنَّ فِيهَا لُوطًا ۚ قَالُوا۟ نَحْنُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَن فِيهَا ۖ لَنُنَجِّيَنَّهُۥ وَأَهْلَهُۥٓ إِلَّا ٱمْرَأَتَهُۥ كَانَتْ مِنَ ٱلْغَـٰبِرِينَ (۳۲)
He said, ‘Lot is in it.’ They said, ‘We know better those who are in it. We will surely deliver him and his family, except his wife: she shall be one of those who remain behind.’ (32)

Key Points

By combining this Ayah and Ayah 36 of Surah Adh-Dhariyat, which states: «فما وجدنا فیها غیر بیت من المسلمین» (We found not in it [the city] but one house of those who submitted [to Allah]), it is understood that only Prophet Lut and his children were believers, and even his wife was afflicted by the punishment. It is understood from Ayah 74 of Surah Hud that Prophet Ibrahim requested Allah not to send divine wrath upon the people of Lut; however, this request was not accepted, and the response came: Disregard this matter. «یجادلنا فى قوم لوط... یا ابراهیم اعرض عن هذا انّه قد جاء امر ربّك»(137)

(137) Hud, 74 - 76.

Messages

1- The presence of Allah’s chosen servants (Awliya) in a society prevents the descent of divine wrath. «انّ فیها لوطاً»

2- The Prophets and their followers are safe from divine wrath. «لنُنجّینّه و اهله»

3- The angels know every single individual. «نحن اعلم بمن فیها»

4- Sometimes, men of truth have been in extreme isolation (Ghurba). (Only Lut’s children believed in their father!) «لنُنجّینّه و اهله»

5- Children, during ideological conflict between parents, can choose the path of truth. «و اهله الاّ امراته» (Lut’s children preferred the path of their father over the path of their mother.)

6- A woman has an independent personality and can choose not to be influenced by her husband or the economic and cultural system of the family. «الاّ امراته»

7- Affiliation is not the key to salvation; detachment/piety (Warastegi) is the key to salvation. «لنُنجّینّه و اهله الاّ امراته» (Lut’s wife was affiliated with him, but she herself was not pious/detached.)

8- Social title is not important; faith and performance are the criteria. «الاّ امراته» (Being the wife of a Prophet is not valuable in itself; accompanying and obeying him is valuable.)

9- The wives of the Prophets are not infallible (Ma'sum). «الاّ امراته» (Even among the wives of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله, only some were subject to special divine favors. As we read elsewhere: «فانّ اللَّه أعدّ للمحسنات منكنّ أجرا عظیما»(138)) (138) Al-Ahzab, 29.

Ayah

وَلَمَّآ أَن جَآءَتْ رُسُلُنَا لُوطًا سِىٓءَ بِهِمْ وَضَاقَ بِهِمْ ذَرْعًا وَقَالُوا۟ لَا تَخَفْ وَلَا تَحْزَنْ ۖ إِنَّا مُنَجُّوكَ وَأَهْلَكَ إِلَّا ٱمْرَأَتَكَ كَانَتْ مِنَ ٱلْغَـٰبِرِينَ (۳۳)
And when Our messengers came to Lot, he was distressed on their account and in a predicament for their sake. But they said, ‘Do not be afraid, nor grieve! We shall deliver you and your family, except your wife: she will be one of those who remain behind. (33)

Key Points

The word sī'a (سِى ء) means becoming distressed or unwell. The distress of Prophet Lot (عليه السلام) might have been due to fear of the wicked people assaulting the angelic guests in his house, or due to knowing about the descent of Allah's wrath and punishment upon his people. Dhira' (ذراع) refers to the part of the hand (from the elbow to the fingers). The narrowing of the dhira' is a metaphor for inability and helplessness, because someone whose elbow is short is incapable of performing many tasks. To anyone who is somehow incapacitated and unable to act, they say: «ضاق بهم ذرعاً».(139) «لاتخف» (Do not fear) relates to worry about potential future events, hardships, or bitterness. And «لاتحزن» (Do not grieve) relates to sorrow and grief over past matters.

139) Majma' al-Bahrain.

Messages

1- In a corrupt society, men of God are put in distress and are even worried about protecting their guests and their progeny. «سى ء بهم»

2- The spread of corruption should not make us indifferent. «سى ء بهم و ضاق بهم» (Religious zeal is a prerequisite for faith.) «سى ء بهم»

3- Many worries stem from ignorance of the future. «ضاق بهم ذرعاً و قالوا لاتخف و لاتحزن»

4- Inner worry affects the countenance. The angels, seeing the signs of worry on the face of Prophet Lot (عليه السلام), told him: «لاتخف و لاتحزن»

5- We should save the believers from anxiety and distress with truthful and comforting promises. «لاتخف و لاتحزن انّا منجّوك»

6- When stating facts, we should mention the positive points first. «انّا منجّوك»

7- Allah protects His chosen servants. «انّا منجّوك»

8- Family ties do not save a person; faith and righteous deeds are the key to salvation. «الاّ امراتك»

9- A woman possesses an independent personality and can resist the cultural and economic system of the family or society. «الاّ امراتك»

10- The guide to corruption is also corrupt. «الاّ امراتك» (Lot's wife informed the corrupt individuals that a guest had arrived, so they should go to him for sin.)

Ayahs

إِنَّا مُنزِلُونَ عَلَىٰٓ أَهْلِ هَـٰذِهِ ٱلْقَرْيَةِ رِجْزًا مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَفْسُقُونَ (۳٤)
We are indeed going to bring down upon the people of this town a punishment from the sky because of the transgressions they used to commit.’ (34)
وَلَقَد تَّرَكْنَا مِنْهَآ ءَايَةًۢ بَيِّنَةً لِّقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ (۳٥)
Certainly We have left of it a manifest sign for people who exercise their reason. (35)

Key Points

The meaning of al-Qaryah (the town) is the region of Sodom, where the people of Lot lived and which had a large population. Fisq (transgression) means departing from obedience, the clearest example of which is polytheism (shirk) and disbelief (kufr).

Messages

1- From the sky descends both Divine Mercy (such as rain and revelation) and Divine Wrath and punishment. «رجزاً من السماء»

2- Human actions influence nature. Just as good deeds cause the descent of blessings and mercy, «و لو انّ اهل القرى آمنوا واتّقوا لفتحنا علیهم بركات من السّماء والارض» (140), conversely, sins transform blessings into punishment. «رجزاً من السّماء»

3- The repetition of sin and persistence in it causes the descent of Divine Wrath. «كانوا یفسقون»

4- Sometimes it is necessary to preserve the relics of the past and ancient ruins and not proceed with repair or reconstruction. «تركنا ... آیة»

5- Familiarity with the philosophy of history and the knowledge of past nations is emphasized by the Quran. «آیة بیّنة لقوم یعقلون»

6- Lessons can be learned even from ruins. «آیة... لقوم یعقلون»

7- Taking heed requires precision and reasoning. Negligent individuals, with a superficial view, cannot grasp the realities and take lessons. «لقوم یعقلون»

8- Reasoning and contemplation are prerequisites for guidance and taking lessons. «لقوم یعقلون» (140) Al-A'raf, 96.

Ayah

وَإِلَىٰ مَدْيَنَ أَخَاهُمْ شُعَيْبًا فَقَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَٱرْجُوا۟ ٱلْيَوْمَ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَ وَلَا تَعْثَوْا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مُفْسِدِينَ (۳٦)
And to Midian We sent Shu‘ayb, their brother. He said, ‘O my people! Worship Allah, and expect [to encounter] the Last Day, and do not act wickedly on the earth causing corruption.’ (36)

Key Points

"Madyan" is a city in southwestern Jordan and is now known as "Ma'an." The word "ta'thaw" (تعثوا) is derived from "athā" (عثى), meaning to cause corruption. Prophet Shu'ayb was responsible for guiding two tribes: A: The People of Madyan (Aṣḥāb Madyan), who were destroyed by a heavenly blast (ṣayḥah). B: The People of the Thicket (Aṣḥāb al-Aykah), who were afflicted by the punishment of "Yawm al-Ẓullah" (the Day of the Shade), a canopy of lightning-bearing clouds. (141)

141) Tafsir Atyab al-Bayan.

Messages

1- The Prophets, who are the intellectual, spiritual, and moral fathers of the people, treat them like brothers. "akhāhum Shu'ayban" (their brother Shu'ayb).

2- In inviting people towards Allah, we should not forget emotions (or affection). "Yā qawmi 'budū Allāh" (O my people! Worship Allah).

3- Monotheism (Tawhid) and the Resurrection (Ma'ad) are at the forefront of the Prophets' call. "Worship Allah and hope for the Last Day."

4- Servitude to Allah and faith in the Origin (Mabda') and the Resurrection (Ma'ad) are the basis for avoiding corruption. "Worship Allah and hope for the Last Day, and do not commit mischief."

5- Humans are not infallible (ma'ṣūm) and sometimes fall into error and sin; what is more dangerous is conscious, malicious, and persistent corruption. "Do not commit mischief... as corrupters (mufsidīn)."

Ayah

فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ ٱلرَّجْفَةُ فَأَصْبَحُوا۟ فِى دَارِهِمْ جَـٰثِمِينَ (۳۷)
But they impugned him, whereupon the earthquake seized them, and they lay lifeless prostrate in their homes. (37)

Key Points

The word "rajfah" (رَجفة) means the shaking of the earth. "Arājīf" (اراجیف) are news and statements that cause instability in the people's belief and morale. "Murjifūn" (مُرجفون) refers to those who say or write such things. "Jāthimīn" (جاثمین) means sitting on the knees; it is as if those afflicted by the punishment woke up from sleep due to the earthquake and were in a half-rising state when their appointed time did not grant them respite.

Messages

1- The denial of the prophets is the prelude to Divine wrath. «فكذّبوه فاخذتهم»

2- How can a human being who lacks the power to escape the wrath of Allah deny the truth? «فكذّبوه... فاصبحوا... جاثمین»

Ayah

وَعَادًا وَثَمُودَا۟ وَقَد تَّبَيَّنَ لَكُم مِّن مَّسَـٰكِنِهِمْ ۖ وَزَيَّنَ لَهُمُ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ أَعْمَـٰلَهُمْ فَصَدَّهُمْ عَنِ ٱلسَّبِيلِ وَكَانُوا۟ مُسْتَبْصِرِينَ (۳۸)
And ‘Ad and Thamud, [whose fate] is evident to you from their habitations. Satan made their deeds seem decorous to them, thus he barred them from the way [of Allah], though they used to be perceptive. (38)

Messages

1- Preserving some historical relics is necessary for people to take heed. «تبیّن لكم من مساكنهم»

2- Seeing one's own evil deeds as good is the trap of Satan. «زیّن لهم الشّیطان اعمالهم» (Arrogance, self-aggrandizement, and pride in wealth and power are manifestations of satanic beautification.)

3- Poisoned cultural propaganda and justifications are among the factors leading to deviation from the truth. «زیّن لهم الشّیطان... فصدّهم»

4- Man naturally loves adornment and beauty, and Satan exploits this inner inclination. «زیّن لهم الشّیطان... فصدّهم»

5- Even insightful and aware people are exposed to Satan's deception. «زیّن لهم الشّیطان... و كانوا مستبصرین»

6- Allah does not punish any nation without reason and clear proof. «عاداً و ثمود... و كانوا مستبصرین»

7- People become aware through innate nature (Fitrah), intellect, and the guidance of the prophets, «كانوا مستبصرین» but Satan is not idle. «زیّن لهم الشّیطان... فصدّهم»

Ayah

وَقَـٰرُونَ وَفِرْعَوْنَ وَهَـٰمَـٰنَ ۖ وَلَقَدْ جَآءَهُم مُّوسَىٰ بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ فَٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا كَانُوا۟ سَـٰبِقِينَ (۳۹)
And Korah, Pharaoh, and Haman. Certainly, Moses brought them manifest proofs, but they acted arrogantly in the land; though they could not outmaneuver [Allah]. (39)

Messages

1- The consequence of arrogance is destruction, and their ominous fate is a lesson to be learned. «و قارون و فرعون و هامان ...»

2- The way of Allah is to complete the proof (Hujjah) and then hold (people) accountable. «جاءهم موسى بالبیّنات»

3- The manifestations of arrogance are varied; Qarun was the embodiment of wealth worship, while Pharaoh and Haman were the embodiment of power worship, but all of them shared in transgression, rebellion, and obstinacy against the truth. «و قارون و فرعون و هامان - فاستكبروا»

4- Wealth and power are helpless and ineffective against the power of Allah. «و ما كانوا سابقین»

Ayah

فَكُلًّا أَخَذْنَا بِذَنۢبِهِۦ ۖ فَمِنْهُم مَّنْ أَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِ حَاصِبًا وَمِنْهُم مَّنْ أَخَذَتْهُ ٱلصَّيْحَةُ وَمِنْهُم مَّنْ خَسَفْنَا بِهِ ٱلْأَرْضَ وَمِنْهُم مَّنْ أَغْرَقْنَا ۚ وَمَا كَانَ ٱللَّهُ لِيَظْلِمَهُمْ وَلَـٰكِن كَانُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ (٤٠)
So We seized each [of them] for his sin: among them were those upon whom We unleashed a rain of stones, and among them were those who were seized by the Cry, and among them were those whom We caused the earth to swallow, and among them were those whom We drowned. It was not Allah who wronged them, but it was they who used to wrong themselves. (40)

Key Points

The word "حاصِب" (hāṣib) means a storm accompanied by pebbles or hail, and "خَسف" (khasf) means sinking into the earth. The people of 'Ād were destroyed by a storm, the people of Thamūd by a heavenly cry (ṣayḥah), Qārūn by sinking into the earth, and Pharaoh and Hāmān by drowning in water. Indeed, sometimes Divine punishment occurs through these very natural factors.

Messages

1- In the method of propagation (da'wah), summarizing and listing points after presenting the material and examples is useful and effective. "فكلاًّ اخذنا بذنبه..."

2- The punishment of every nation is commensurate with the sin of that nation. "فكلاًّ اخذنا بذنبه"

3- Not all punishments are deferred until the Day of Judgment. (Some nations were afflicted with punishment in this very world.) "فكلاًّ اخذنا ..."

4- We should not be deceived by Allah's granting of respite, for the consequence of sin is punishment. "فكلاًّ اخذنا بذنبه"

5- Allah's power is open for various types of torment. "حاصباً - خسفنا - اغرقنا"

6- Divine wrath is just. "و ما كان اللَّه لیظلمهم"

7- The destiny of every person is tied to his own deeds. "كانوا أنفسهم یظلمون"

8- Arrogance against the truth is oppression against oneself. "كانوا أنفسهم یظلمون"

9- Uglier than oppression is the persistence of oppression. "كانوا أنفسهم یظلمون"

Ayah

مَثَلُ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّخَذُوا۟ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ أَوْلِيَآءَ كَمَثَلِ ٱلْعَنكَبُوتِ ٱتَّخَذَتْ بَيْتًا ۖ وَإِنَّ أَوْهَنَ ٱلْبُيُوتِ لَبَيْتُ ٱلْعَنكَبُوتِ ۖ لَوْ كَانُوا۟ يَعْلَمُونَ (٤۱)
The parable of those who take guardians instead of Allah is that of the spider that takes a home, and indeed the frailest of homes is the home of a spider, had they known! (41)

Key Points

Due to this Ayah, this Surah has been named Al-Ankabut (The Spider). It is narrated from Plato that the fly is the most greedy insect, settling on sourness, sweetness, filth, and wounds for its sustenance; but the spider spins (its web) in a corner and is content. It is interesting that Allah makes the most active and greedy animal the prey of the most reclusive animal. The fly goes to the spider with its own feet, and the spider hunts the fly with its threads.

Messages

1- Using parables is one of the best methods in teaching and training, «مثل الّذین ...» And the best examples are those that are tangible for all segments of people, in all times and places. «كمثل العنكبوت»

2- The foundation of polytheism (Shirk) is weak and baseless, like the spider's house. «مثل الّذین... كمثل العنكبوت...»

3- The spider builds its house in desolate places; similarly, polytheism takes root and affects souls that are far from Allah. «دون اللَّه - كمثل العنكبوت»

4- The spider's house only bears the name of a house; those besides Allah are nothing more than names. «دون اللَّه - أوهن البیوت»

5- The spider imagines in its world that it has a house and that its house is resistant to accidents; the polytheist suffers from the same delusion. «أوهن البیوت»

6- Divine guardianship (Wilayah) has a solid foundation and an iron structure, «كانّهم بنیان مرصوص»(142) but the guardianship of others besides Allah is extremely weak. «أوهن البیوت»

7- Sometimes motives, delusions, and affections prevent the understanding of the truth. «لو كانوا یعلمون» (As it is said: «حبّ الشى ء یعمى و یصمّ» [The love of a thing makes one blind and deaf], certain attachments make a person blind and deaf.)

8- Polytheists turn to others besides Allah out of ignorance and unawareness. «لو كانوا یعلمون»(142) Saff, 4.

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ مَا يَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِهِۦ مِن شَىْءٍ ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (٤۲)
Allah indeed knows whatever thing they invoke besides Him, and He is the All-mighty, the All-wise. (42)
وَتِلْكَ ٱلْأَمْثَـٰلُ نَضْرِبُهَا لِلنَّاسِ ۖ وَمَا يَعْقِلُهَآ إِلَّا ٱلْعَـٰلِمُونَ (٤۳)
We draw these parables for mankind; but no one grasps them except those who have knowledge. (43)

Messages

1- Man's non-Divine inclinations are under Allah's supervision; we must prepare ourselves for accountability. «انّ اللّه یعلم ما یدعون...»

2- The comparison of polytheism (shirk) to a spider's web originates from Allah's knowledge. «كمثل العنكبوت... انّ اللّه یعلم ما یدعون...»

3- Instead of relying on others besides Allah, which is weak and baseless like the spider's house, we should rely upon the strong and invincible Allah. «و هو العزیز الحكیم»

4- Our attention to others does not harm Allah. «و هو العزیز»

5- Allah's Might is accompanied by wisdom. (It is not a power and firmness devoid of logic and wisdom.) «العزیز الحكیم»

6- The parables of the Quran are profound, and in addition to the general public, scholars must also reflect upon their depth. «نضربها للنّاس و ما یعقلها الاّ العالمون»

7- Allah encourages people to acquire knowledge in order to grasp the subtle points of the Quran. «و ما یعقلها الاّ العالمون»

Ayah

خَلَقَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ بِٱلْحَقِّ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَةً لِّلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (٤٤)
Allah created the heavens and the earth with consummate wisdom. There is indeed a sign in that for the faithful. (44)

Key Points

Other than Allah, everyone and everything is weak, unstable, and perishable, like the spider's house. But Allah: He is also All-Knowing. «یعلم ما یدعون» (previous Ayah) He is also Mighty and Wise. «و هو العزیز الحكیم» He is also the Creator of existence. «خلق اللَّه ...»

Messages

1- The creation of the universe is purposeful; «بالحقّ». Our view of the universe must also be purposeful. «لآیةً»

2- Materialists stop at phenomena, but monotheists pass through phenomena and reach the knowledge of Allah. «لآیةً»

3- Faith is one of the conditions for understanding the realities of existence. «انّ فى ذلك لآیة للمؤمنین»

Ayah

ٱتْلُ مَآ أُوحِىَ إِلَيْكَ مِنَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ وَأَقِمِ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ ۖ إِنَّ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ تَنْهَىٰ عَنِ ٱلْفَحْشَآءِ وَٱلْمُنكَرِ ۗ وَلَذِكْرُ ٱللَّهِ أَكْبَرُ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ مَا تَصْنَعُونَ (٤٥)
Recite what has been revealed to you of the Book, and maintain the prayer. Indeed the prayer restrains from indecent and wrongful conduct, and the remembrance of Allah is surely greater. And Allah knows whatever [deeds] you do. (45)

Key Points

In this Ayah, Allah has commanded His Prophet to recite the Quran and perform the Prayer side by side, and this is because the Quran and the Prayer are two sources of strength. Allah, Who informs His Prophet of a heavy responsibility, «انّا سنلقى علیك قولاً ثقیلاً»(143), tells him: To carry out this heavy responsibility, seek help from two sources of strength: One is the recitation of the Quran. «و رَتّل القرآن ترتیلا»(144) And the other is the night prayer. «اِنّ ناشئة الیل هى اَشدّ وَطاً و أقوم قیلاً»(145) Of course, besides this Ayah, the Prayer and the Quran have been mentioned together many times; including: «یتلون كتاب اللّه و اقاموا الصّلاة»(146) and «یمسّكون بالكتاب و اقاموا الصّلاة»(147)

Several meanings have been stated for the phrase «لذكر اللّه اكبر»: A: Prayer is the greatest remembrance (Dhikr) of Allah. Due to the Ayah «أقم الصّلاة لذكرى»(148), which considers Prayer to be the remembrance of Allah. B: The remembrance of Allah (and presence of heart) is superior to the outward form of the Prayer. C: The remembrance of Allah is superior to all other deeds for man. D: The remembrance of Allah, for preventing indecency (Fahsha) and evil (Munkar), is superior to any other leverage. E: Allah's remembrance of you is greater than your remembrance of Him. (Of course, this meaning is derived by considering the narrations under the Ayah «فاذكرونى اذكركم»(149).(150))

It was said to the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله: So-and-so both prays and commits sins. He said: «انّ صلاته تنهاه یوماً»(151) His Prayer will eventually save him one day.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Whoever wishes to know whether his Prayer is accepted or rejected should see whether his Prayer has restrained him from indecency and evil or not. Then the Imam عليه السلام said: «فبقدر ما منعته قبلت منه»(152) To the extent that the Prayer restrains a person from evils, to that same extent it is accepted.

143) Muzammil, 5. 144) Muzammil, 4. 145) Muzammil, 6. 146) Fatir, 29. 147) A'raf, 170. 148) Taha, 14. 149) Baqarah, 152. 150) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 151) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 152) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan.

Messages

  1. Mere familiarity with the concepts, recitation, and teaching of the Quran is not enough; action is necessary. «اُتلُ - أقِم»
  2. The Quran and the Prayer are at the head of educational programs. «اُتلُ - أقِم»
  3. The Prophet's relationship with the people is through the recitation of the Quranic verses and the conveyance of Allah's commands to them, and His relationship with Allah is through worship and Prayer. «اُتلُ - أقِم»
  4. In propagation and guidance, we should explain the wisdom and effects of religious commands. «أقِم الصّلاة انّ الصّلاة تنهى عن الفحشاء و المنكر»
  5. Before Allah, the Prayer holds a special greatness and status. «أقم الصّلاة اِنّ الصّلاة» (Repetition of the word "Salat")
  6. The reformative role of the Prayer in the individual and society is not speculative or suggestive, but definite. «اِنّ الصّلاة» (The word "Inna" and the nominal sentence)
  7. If a person's Prayer does not restrain him from indecency and evil, he should doubt the acceptance of his Prayer. «اِنّ الصّلاة تنهى»
  8. The expansion of good deeds and virtue (Ma'ruf) naturally prevents the growth of evil (Munkar). «الصّلاة تنهى»
  9. Allah's knowledge guarantees the correct implementation of His commands. «واللّه یعلم ما تصنعون»
  10. Various secrets, symbols, arts, and subtleties are hidden within the Prayer. «ما تصنعون»

Prayer and Restraint from Evils

Question: How does the Prayer restrain the individual and society from indecency and evil?

Answer: 1. The root of all evils is heedlessness (Ghaflah), and Allah, in Ayah 179 of Surah Al-A'raf, considers the heedless person worse than an animal. «اولئك كالانعام بل هم اضلّ اولئك هم الغافلون» And since the Prayer is the remembrance of Allah and the best means of removing heedlessness, it eliminates the grounds for evils.

  1. Establishing the Prayer and having a divine color prevents one from taking on a satanic color. Just as someone who wears white clothes is unwilling to sit in a dirty and polluted place.

  2. Alongside the Prayer, Zakat (alms) is usually recommended, which frees the individual from the evil of stinginess and indifference towards the deprived, and frees society from the evil of poverty, which is the ground for turning towards evils.

  3. The Prayer possesses rules and commands, the observance of each of which restrains a person from many sins, for example:

  4. The condition that the place and clothing of the worshipper must be lawful (Halal) restrains a person from violating the rights of others.

  5. Observing the condition of purity (Tahara) of the water for ablution (Wudu), the place, the clothing, and the body of the worshipper keeps a person away from pollution and carelessness.
  6. The condition of sincerity (Ikhlas) restrains a person from the evil of polytheism (Shirk), hypocrisy (Riya), and seeking fame (Sum'ah).
  7. The condition of the Qibla restrains a person from the evil of aimlessness and turning attention everywhere.
  8. Ruku' (bowing) and Sujud (prostration) restrain a person from the evil of arrogance (Takabbur).
  9. Attention to appropriate covering in the Prayer protects a person from the evil of nudity and shamelessness.
  10. Attention to the justice (Adalah) of the Imam of the congregation causes individuals to distance themselves from transgression (Fisq) and sin.
  11. Congregational Prayer (Salat al-Jama'ah) saves a person from undue isolation.
  12. The rules and conditions of the Congregational Prayer revive many values, including: being social, not preceding the leader, not lagging behind society, silence in the face of the rightful speech of the Imam of the congregation, order and discipline, valuing pious individuals, avoiding division, avoiding reprehensible racial, regional, and political tendencies, and presence on the scene—the abandonment of any of which is an evil.
  13. The necessity of reciting Surah Al-Hamd (Al-Fatiha) in every Prayer expresses man's relationship with the Creator of the universe, «ربّ العالمین», with gratitude, devotion, and humility before Him, «ایّاك نعبد», with reliance and seeking help from Him, «ایّاك نستعین», with attention to and remembrance of the Resurrection, «مالك یوم الدین», with the infallible leaders and divine saints, «انعمت علیهم», with dissociation from corrupt leaders, «غیرالمغضوب», and man's relationship with other people in society, «نعبد و نستعین»—heedlessness of any of which is an evil, or a precursor to a great evil.

Ayah

۞ وَلَا تُجَـٰدِلُوٓا۟ أَهْلَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ إِلَّا بِٱلَّتِى هِىَ أَحْسَنُ إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ مِنْهُمْ ۖ وَقُولُوٓا۟ ءَامَنَّا بِٱلَّذِىٓ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْنَا وَأُنزِلَ إِلَيْكُمْ وَإِلَـٰهُنَا وَإِلَـٰهُكُمْ وَٰحِدٌ وَنَحْنُ لَهُۥ مُسْلِمُونَ (٤٦)
Do not argue with the People of the Book except in a manner which is best, except such of them as are wrongdoers, and say, ‘We believe in what has been sent down to us and in what has been sent down to you; our God and your God is one [and the same], and to Him do we submit.’ (46)

Key Points

Jidāl (disputation) literally means intertwining or twisting together. Jidāl refers to the conversations between two people who engage in a verbal conflict in order to overcome one another. Jidāl aḥsan (the best disputation) refers to a conversation that is not accompanied by taunts, mockery, insults, or humiliation, but rather is based on reasoning, affection, and motivated by reformative intentions. It is understood from the word «قولوا» (say/speak) following the recommendation for Jidāl aḥsan that the tone of this very verse is an example of Jidāl aḥsan. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام introduced the final verses of Sūrat Yā Sīn as an example of Jidāl aḥsan, where a person powdered a decayed bone and said: «مَن یحیى العظام» (Who will revive the bones?) The Quran replied: «قل یُحییها الّذى أنشأها اوّل مرّة» (Say: He will revive them Who produced them the first time)—the One Who first gave it life will revive it again.(153)

153) Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Conversation with non-Muslims must also be conducted in the best manner. «لاتجادلوا اهل الكتاب الاّ بالّتى هى أحسن»

2- A disputation is acceptable only if its content, method, and wording are aḥsan (best). «بالّتى هى أحسن»

3- The exchange of views and proper dialogue among schools of thought, cultures, and nations is accepted in Islam. «بالّتى هى أحسن»

4- We must know our audience and deal with each group in an appropriate manner. Logic, dialogue, and gentleness are ineffective for oppressors. «الاّ الّذین ظلموا»

5- Not all opponents of Islam are oppressors. «الاّ الّذین ظلموا منهم»

6- In dialogues, before expressing our specific beliefs and opinions, we should mention what is agreed upon. «الهنا و الهكم واحد» (Our God and your God is One.)

7- Faith alone is not sufficient; submission and obedience are also necessary. «آمنّا... نحن له مسلمون» (We have believed... and we are Muslims [submitting] to Him.)

Ayah

وَكَذَٰلِكَ أَنزَلْنَآ إِلَيْكَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ ۚ فَٱلَّذِينَ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُمُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِۦ ۖ وَمِنْ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ مَن يُؤْمِنُ بِهِۦ ۚ وَمَا يَجْحَدُ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَآ إِلَّا ٱلْكَـٰفِرُونَ (٤۷)
Thus We have sent down the Book to you; those to whom We have given the Book believe in it, and of these there are some who believe in it, and none contests Our signs except the faithless. (47)

Key Points

"Juhūd" (denial) is the rejection of something the heart accepts, or the acceptance of something the heart rejects.

Messages

1- The Quran invites the followers of all previous religions to Islam. «فالّذین آتیناهم الكتاب یؤمنون به»

2- Divine guidance is certain and necessary, even if not all people accept guidance. «انزلنا - و مِن هؤلاء مَن یؤمن به»

3- Those who become familiar with the Quran but do not accept it are afflicted by a type of disbelief (kufr). «و ما یجحد بآیاتنا الاّ الكافرون»

Ayah

وَمَا كُنتَ تَتْلُوا۟ مِن قَبْلِهِۦ مِن كِتَـٰبٍ وَلَا تَخُطُّهُۥ بِيَمِينِكَ ۖ إِذًا لَّٱرْتَابَ ٱلْمُبْطِلُونَ (٤۸)
You did not use to recite any scripture before it, nor did you write it with your right hand, for then the impugners would have been skeptical. (48)

Messages

1- We should not be proud of our reading and writing; sometimes, by Divine Will, an unlettered and uneducated individual changes the culture of humanity. «و ما كنتَ تتلوا»

My beloved, who did not go to school and did not write a line,

Became the teacher of a hundred instructors through a glance.

2- One of the proofs of the truthfulness of the Quran is the unlettered nature (ummiyyah) of the Prophet of Islam. «و لاتخطّهُ...»

3- Vigilance and preventing the misuse and excuse-making of opponents are necessary. «اذاً لارتاب المبطلون»

4- The revelation of the Quran upon the unlettered Prophet, who does not know how to read or write, is one of the ways Allah completes His argument (Hujjah) against the people. «اذاً لارتاب المبطلون»

5- Abandoning the truth and seeking grounds for doubt and skepticism is a sign of human sickness and incorrect motives. «لارتاب المبطلون»

Ayah

بَلْ هُوَ ءَايَـٰتٌۢ بَيِّنَـٰتٌ فِى صُدُورِ ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْعِلْمَ ۚ وَمَا يَجْحَدُ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَآ إِلَّا ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ (٤۹)
Indeed, it is [present as] manifest signs in the breasts of those who have been given knowledge, and none contests Our signs except wrongdoers. (49)

Key Points

By comparing Ayah 47, which considers only the disbelievers as deniers of Allah's signs, «و ما یجحد بآیاتنا الاّ الكافرون», and this Ayah, which considers only the wrongdoers as deniers of the signs, «و ما یجحد بآیاتنا الاّ الظالمون», it can be concluded that disbelief (kufr) is a type of wrongdoing (zulm) or vice versa. Approximately twenty narrations (hadith) have been cited in exegetical books stating that the intent of «اوتوا العلم» (those given knowledge) in this Ayah are the infallible Imams علیهم السلام.

Messages

1- Although some verses of the Qur’an are ambiguous (mutashābih), the totality of its verses is clear and illuminating. «آیاتٌ بیّنات»

2- The sign of true knowledge is the acceptance of clear divine signs. «فى صدور الّذین اوتوا العلم»

3- Although man is commanded to strive and endeavor to reach his goal, knowledge is a divine gift. «اوتوا العلم»

4- The verses of the Qur’an settle upon the hearts of the people of knowledge, and it is this group that perceives its truthfulness with their entire being. «الّذین اوتوا العلم»

5- A clear example of wrongdoing (zulm) is cultural oppression and undue denial. «و ما یجحد بآیاتنا الاّ الظلمون»

6- If a person denies what he understands, it is a type of wrongdoing. «الاّ الظالمون»

Ayah

وَقَالُوا۟ لَوْلَآ أُنزِلَ عَلَيْهِ ءَايَـٰتٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦ ۖ قُلْ إِنَّمَا ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتُ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ وَإِنَّمَآ أَنَا۠ نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ (٥٠)
They say, ‘Why has not some sign been sent down to him from his Lord?’ Say, ‘These signs are only from Allah, and I am only a manifest warner.’ (50)

Key Points

People requested material miracles from the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله (such as the staff of Moses, the White Hand, etc.), unaware that the miracle of every era must be appropriate for that era. Therefore, the miracle of the Seal of the Prophets is of the nature of speech and discourse. Truly, given the existence of a miracle like the Quran, is the request for another miracle not ingratitude?

Messages

1- The fault-finding of stubborn individuals is endless. (Even after seeing various types of miracles, they still request another miracle.) «لولا انزل»

2- The Prophet resisted the fault-finders and responded to them, because a miracle is a wise and Divine act, not a plaything for the fault-finders. «قل انّما الایات عند اللّه»

3- Warning and admonition, due to their power to remove heedlessness, are stronger and more effective than glad tidings and hope. «انّما انا نذیر» and He did not say: «انّما انا بشیر»

4- The warning of the Prophets is explicit and clear. (Not through allusion, ambiguity, or insinuation.) «مبین»

Ayah

أَوَلَمْ يَكْفِهِمْ أَنَّآ أَنزَلْنَا عَلَيْكَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ يُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَرَحْمَةً وَذِكْرَىٰ لِقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ (٥۱)
Does it not suffice them that We have sent down to you the Book which is recited to them? There is indeed in that a mercy and admonition for a people who have faith. (51)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, we read that the disbelievers requested miracles from the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله similar to the miracles of Prophet Moses and Jesus; this Ayah is the response to them.

Messages

1- The Quran is a comprehensive and complete book, and it answers and fulfills all spiritual needs in some way. «اولم یكفهم»

2- Both the Prophet of Allah is «رحمة للعالمین» (154) and His Book is. (The word «رحمةً» is indefinite (nakirah) and carries tanwin, which is a sign of its greatness and magnificence.)

3- The Quran is a source of mercy and distance from heedlessness. «لرحمةً و ذكرى»

4- The condition for receiving mercy and accepting divine reminders is faith. «لقوم یؤمنون» (154) Al-Anbiya, 107.

Ayah

قُلْ كَفَىٰ بِٱللَّهِ بَيْنِى وَبَيْنَكُمْ شَهِيدًا ۖ يَعْلَمُ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ بِٱلْبَـٰطِلِ وَكَفَرُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْخَـٰسِرُونَ (٥۲)
Say, ‘Allah suffices as a witness between me and you: He knows whatever there is in the heavens and the earth. Those who put faith in falsehood and defy Allah—it is they who are the losers.’ (52)

Key Points

This Ayah provides comfort to the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله and serves as a threat to the stubborn and persistent fault-finders, because Allah is a Witness to what transpires between the Prophet and the disbelievers, and He holds everything accountable.

Messages

1- If argumentation with the opponent is fruitless, cease the discussion and entrust him to Allah. «قل كفى»

2- Allah confirms the Prophet's truthfulness and bears witness to it through the revelation of the Quran and the glad tidings of the Prophet of Islam's coming in previous heavenly Books. «شهیدا»

3- Allah is aware of everything, so why the stubbornness and fault-finding?! «یعلم ما فى السموات...»

4- Abandoning faith and spirituality and becoming attached to other than Him is stepping onto the path of disbelief, falsehood, futility, and deviation, the ultimate result of which is true loss. «آمنوا بالباطل ... هم الخاسرون»

5- The disbelievers are the true losers. «كفروا باللّه اولئك هم الخاسرون

Ayahs

وَيَسْتَعْجِلُونَكَ بِٱلْعَذَابِ ۚ وَلَوْلَآ أَجَلٌ مُّسَمًّى لَّجَآءَهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ وَلَيَأْتِيَنَّهُم بَغْتَةً وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ (٥۳)
They ask you to hasten the punishment. Yet were it not for a specified time, the punishment would have surely overtaken them. Surely, it will overtake them suddenly while they are unaware. (53)
يَسْتَعْجِلُونَكَ بِٱلْعَذَابِ وَإِنَّ جَهَنَّمَ لَمُحِيطَةٌۢ بِٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (٥٤)
They ask you to hasten the punishment, and indeed hell will besiege the faithless (54)

Key Points

It has been mentioned several times in the Quran: the disbelievers who were threatened with divine punishment, due to disbelief or mockery, constantly requested immediate punishment from their Prophet; this haste shown by the disbelievers has been criticized in all instances. There are blessings in the delay of divine punishment and wrath, including: A: It is an opportunity for repentance. B: The emergence of righteous children from misguided fathers. C: The testing of people in their religiosity and steadfastness.

Messages

1- Divine grace or wrath is wise, calculated, and planned, and does not change due to the haste of this person or that. «یستعجلونك»

2- The intellectual decline of man is such that he is willing to be destroyed rather than accept the truth. «یستعجلونك بالعذاب»

3- The affairs of this world have calculated and specified timings. «اجلٌ مسّمىً»

4- Allah’s sudden wrath is meant for our perpetual readiness. «بغتةً»

5- Divine wrath is not predictable. «لا یشعرون»
