6- Although the disbelievers expect immediate punishment from the Prophet due to disbelief or mockery, they should know that the encompassing of the disbelievers by Hell is certain. «لمحیطة بالكافرین»

Ayah

يَوْمَ يَغْشَىٰهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ مِن فَوْقِهِمْ وَمِن تَحْتِ أَرْجُلِهِمْ وَيَقُولُ ذُوقُوا۟ مَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (٥٥)
on the day when the punishment envelopes them, from above them and from under their feet, and He will say, ‘Taste what you used to do!’ (55)

Messages

1- The punishment of Hell is comprehensive. «فوقهم... تحت ارجلهم»

2- The punishment of Hell is the embodiment of our own actions. «ذوقوا ما كنتم تعملون»

3- Man is responsible for his own destiny. «ما كنتم تعملون»

4- The comprehensive punishment of Hell is due to the persistence of our wrongful deeds. «ما كنتم تعملون»

Ayah

يَـٰعِبَادِىَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِنَّ أَرْضِى وَٰسِعَةٌ فَإِيَّـٰىَ فَٱعْبُدُونِ (٥٦)
O My servants who have faith! My earth is indeed vast. So worship [only] Me. (56)

Key Points

Islam has made migration (Hijrah) obligatory for a group of people. Those who live in regions of disbelief (kufr) and tyranny (taghut-stricken), who are afflicted by oppression and suffocation, and who find their growth and salvation in migration, must migrate. However, in the face of this duty, temptations (or whisperings/doubts) from within and without are cast upon man, and the following verses provide answers to these temptations, including:

  • The danger of death. Man says: If I migrate, death might come to me. The next verse says: Wherever you are, you shall taste death.
  • Detachment from one's dwelling; which is difficult for man and tempts him not to migrate. Verse 58 answers that instead of the dwelling of this world, We reward the migrants with chambers in Paradise.
  • The hardships of migration; which sometimes prevent the people of faith from migrating. In verse 59, He says: This temptation must be eliminated through patience (sabr) and reliance (tawakkul) upon Allah.
  • Securing livelihood. Someone considering migration might say: Migration will deprive me of income and sustenance (rizq). Verse 60 answers: Allah is the Provider of sustenance, and He provides even for creatures that cannot carry their own sustenance, let alone migrants who are people of work and effort.

Migration is the ground for the emergence of sincerity (Ikhlas). Those who do not migrate and consider themselves dependent on an individual, a party, a region, a tribe, resources, or the expectations of others, will inevitably be drawn away from sincerity by regional, racial, tribal issues, narrow-mindedness arising from jealousies, and the negative rivalries of insiders and outsiders. Migration to regions where the aforementioned issues do not exist is the best ground for the emergence of sincerity.

Imam Baqir عليه السلام said regarding this verse: Do not obey the people of corruption among the rulers, and if you fear that they will cause discord (fitnah) and deviation in your religion, then migrate. «لاتطیعوا اهل الفسق من الملوك فان خفتموهم اَن یفتنكم عن دینكم فانّ ارضى واسعة»(155)

155) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- We should endure the hardships and bitterness of migration with the loving call of Allah, for He has special favor upon these believers. «یا عبادى»

2- In choosing a dwelling, we should make the success of worship the central focus. «انّ ارضى واسعة فایّاى فاعبدون»

3- Servitude to Allah must be continuous. «یا عبادى... فاعبدون» (Since you are servants, continue to serve so that you may reach higher ranks.)

4- Migration is valuable only when it is under the shadow of faith. «الّذین آمنوا انّ ارضى واسعة»

5- Travel and journeying must be purposeful. «انّ ارضى واسعة فایّاى فاعبدون»

6- We should migrate to preserve religion and escape from tyrants (taghuts). «ارضى واسعة فایّاى فاعبدون»

7- Detachment from the land is a prelude to detachment from all kinds of dependencies and attachment to Allah. «واسعة فایّاى»

8- The excuse of those who deviate due to attachment to a place or region is not accepted, because there is always a suitable space on earth for fulfilling religious duties. «ارضى واسعة فایّاى فاعبدون»

Ayah

كُلُّ نَفْسٍ ذَآئِقَةُ ٱلْمَوْتِ ۖ ثُمَّ إِلَيْنَا تُرْجَعُونَ (٥۷)
Every soul shall taste death. Then you shall be brought back to Us. (57)

Key Points

Perhaps this Ayah is related to the previous Ayah, addressing the question: what is the duty if death overtakes a person during migration (Hijrah)? This Ayah answers: where migration is necessary, do not fear death, because death is the inevitable destiny of everyone; but it is not the end of the matter. You will return to Us, and We will grant you the reward for migration and for dying in the path of migration, and We will punish the oppressors who forced you to migrate, even if the realization of that reward and punishment takes a long time. (The word «ثمّ» [thumma] indicates a long duration of time.)

Messages

1- Death has no exception and is for everyone. «كلّ نفس ذائقة الموت»

2- Death is an existential matter, not annihilation. «ذائقة الموت - ترجعون»

3- Death is not the end of the matter; it is the return to the Origin. «الینا ترجعون»

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ لَنُبَوِّئَنَّهُم مِّنَ ٱلْجَنَّةِ غُرَفًا تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا ۚ نِعْمَ أَجْرُ ٱلْعَـٰمِلِينَ (٥۸)
Those who have faith and do righteous deeds, We will surely settle them in the lofty abodes of paradise, with streams running in them, to remain in them [forever]. How excellent is the reward of the workers! (58)

Key Points

Perhaps this Ayah refers to Ayah 56, which considers migration (Hijrah) a clear example of righteous deed (Amal Salih); meaning, those who migrate from their dwelling in this world for sacred goals, Allah will secure their dwelling in the Hereafter. The word «تَبَوّء» (tabawwu') means providing a permanent dwelling, and «غرفه» (ghurfah) refers to a high place that overlooks its surroundings. «الصالحات» (As-Salihat) is a plural noun that comes with the definite article (Alif and Lam), meaning one should perform all kinds of good deeds.

Messages

1- Worthy deeds are valuable alongside faith. «آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات»

2- The condition for attaining Paradise is faith and righteous deed. «آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات لنبوّئنهم من الجنّة»

3- Allah has guaranteed Paradise for the believers. (The word «لنبوّئنهم» uses the Lam and Nun of emphasis, which indicates certainty.)

4- The chambers (ghuraf) of Paradise are extremely magnificent. (The indefinite form «غرفاً» accompanied by tanwin is a sign of greatness and majesty.)

5- The rivers of Paradise are constantly flowing. («تجرى» [tajri] is a present tense verb, indicating permanence and continuity.)

6- The basis for attaining Paradise and its blessings is action (deed). «نعم اجر العاملین»

Ayahs

ٱلَّذِينَ صَبَرُوا۟ وَعَلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ يَتَوَكَّلُونَ (٥۹)
—Those who are patient and who put their trust in their Lord. (59)
وَكَأَيِّن مِّن دَآبَّةٍ لَّا تَحْمِلُ رِزْقَهَا ٱللَّهُ يَرْزُقُهَا وَإِيَّاكُمْ ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ (٦٠)
How many an animal there is that does not carry its own provision. Allah provides them and you, and He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing. (60)

Key Points

The word taḥmil (تحمل) is derived from the root ḥamālah (حمالة), meaning guarantee, sponsorship, or taking responsibility. It is possible that the intent of "carrying its provision" (ḥaml al-rizq) is storing it for the future. In Ayah 56, migration was commanded. In this Ayah, [Allah] states: Migrants in the way of Allah should not worry about their provision and livelihood. The God Who provides sustenance and livelihood to countless weak creatures will also provide your provision.

Messages

1- Patience (ṣabr) and reliance (tawakkul) are two clear examples of righteous deeds. «عملوا الصّالحات... الّذین صبروا و... یتوكّلون»

2- The Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) of Allah is the basis for reliance upon Him. «على ربّهم یتوكّلون»

3- Psychological pressures and life difficulties are obstacles in the path of the righteous, which must be overcome through patience and reliance. «صبروا - یتوكّلون»

4- The secret to success and felicity lies in four things:

A: Faith and motivation. «آمنوا»

B: Work and effort. «عملوا»

C: Continuity and steadfastness. «صبروا»

D: Reliance upon Allah, against temptations and worries. «على ربّهم یتوكّلون»

5- Reliance upon Allah must be accompanied by the utilization of full capacity and inner steadfastness. («صبروا» refers to the past, meaning they have already utilized their full capacity, and «یتوكّلون» refers to the future, meaning they rely upon Allah against future and unforeseen dangers and events.)

6- To strengthen faith and reliance upon Allah, we should pay attention to Divine favors towards other creatures. «و كایّن من دابّة»

7- Provision and livelihood are not dependent on cleverness. «لا تحمل رزقها اللّه یرزقها و ایاكم»

8- For Allah, providing provision to weak creatures and to healthy, capable humans is the same. «یرزقها و ایّاكم»

9- The knowledge and awareness of Allah guarantee the provision of creatures. «السمیع العلیم»

Ayah

وَلَئِن سَأَلْتَهُم مَّنْ خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ وَسَخَّرَ ٱلشَّمْسَ وَٱلْقَمَرَ لَيَقُولُنَّ ٱللَّهُ ۖ فَأَنَّىٰ يُؤْفَكُونَ (٦۱)
If you ask them, ‘Who created the heavens and the earth, and who has disposed the sun and the moon?’ They will surely say, ‘Allah.’ Then where do they stray? (61)

Key Points

"Ifk" means turning something away from its true form; that is, they knowingly distort the truth. The intent behind the subjugation of the sun and the moon is their subjugation within an orbit that is beneficial for us. (156)

156) Tafsir Rahnama.

Messages

1- By posing questions about the origin of existence, we should both awaken consciences and better gauge the level of people's faith and belief. «ولئن سألتهم»

2- In theological discussions, we should use clear and tangible examples. (The heavens, the earth, the sun, and the moon) «السموات والارض ...»

3- The polytheists knew the Creator of existence was "Allah" but attributed a role to other things in their destiny. «لیقولن اللَّه»

4- The inclination toward truth is innate, and the deviation of the polytheists is contrary to nature and due to external influences. «یؤفكون» is used in the passive form, meaning being led astray, as if external suggestion leads them to deviation.

Ayah

ٱللَّهُ يَبْسُطُ ٱلرِّزْقَ لِمَن يَشَآءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِۦ وَيَقْدِرُ لَهُۥٓ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمٌ (٦۲)
Allah expands the provision for whomever He wishes of His servants, and tightens it for him. Indeed Allah has knowledge of all things. (62)

Key Points

The reduction or increase of provision (rizq) for individuals by Allah is knowledgeable, wise, and based on visible and invisible criteria. We read in a hadith: The welfare of some of Allah's servants lies in the expansion of provision, and if they are placed in straitened circumstances of livelihood, they become corrupt. Conversely, the well-being of another group lies in being in straitened circumstances, for if they experience abundance, they are led to corruption. (157)

(157) Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan.

Messages

1- The reduction or increase of provision (rizq) is not accidental. «اللّه یبسط... و یقدر»

2- Our duty is work and effort; however, the amount of income and provision is not in our control. «اللّه یبسط»

3- The Divine Will and decree are conscious and knowledgeable. «لمن یشاء... بكلّ شى ء علیم»

Ayah

وَلَئِن سَأَلْتَهُم مَّن نَّزَّلَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءً فَأَحْيَا بِهِ ٱلْأَرْضَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَوْتِهَا لَيَقُولُنَّ ٱللَّهُ ۚ قُلِ ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ۚ بَلْ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لَا يَعْقِلُونَ (٦۳)
And if you ask them, ‘Who sends down water from the sky, with which He revives the earth after its death?’ They will surely say, ‘Allah.’ Say, ‘All praise belongs to Allah!’ But most of them do not exercise their reason. (63)

Key Points

The praise (Hamd) offered by the Prophet of Islam might be for the purpose of completing the argument (Hujjah) against the disbelievers; meaning, now that you verbally acknowledge the Creatorship of Allah, thank God (Allah) that the proof is complete against you.

Messages

1- Posing a question is a means of bringing diverse thoughts, cultures, and beliefs closer together. «لئن سألتهم»

2- We should not abandon the misguided to their own devices; we must approach them using various cultural methods. «سألتهم»

3- Water is the source of life for the earth. «فاحیا به الارض»

4- The earth without vegetation is dead. «بعد موتها»

5- The method of guidance used by the Quran is the utilization of natural and tangible occurrences. (The start of spring with the descent of rain) «فاحیا به الارض بعد موتها»

6- Knowledge of God (Allah) is innate (Fitri), and if the dust of sin and deviation is wiped away from the innate nature (Fitrah) of the misguided, you will find their clear confessions. «لئن سألتهم - لیقولنّ اللَّه»

7- One of the great blessings for which we must offer thanks is the light of faith and innate nature (Fitrah). «قل الحمد للّه»

8- The light of innate nature saves man only when it develops through intellect and reason. «اكثرهم لایعقلون»

9- In judgments, we should not focus on the minority or the majority, for perhaps the minority is upon the truth (Haqq) and the majority is upon falsehood (Batil). «اكثرهم لایعقلون»

10- Performing actions without thought and reflection leads to reproach. «اكثرهم لا یعقلون»

Ayah

وَمَا هَـٰذِهِ ٱلْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَآ إِلَّا لَهْوٌ وَلَعِبٌ ۚ وَإِنَّ ٱلدَّارَ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةَ لَهِىَ ٱلْحَيَوَانُ ۚ لَوْ كَانُوا۟ يَعْلَمُونَ (٦٤)
The life of this world is nothing but diversion and play, but the abode of the Hereafter is indeed Life (itself), had they known! (64)

Key Points

"Lahu" refers to amusements that distract a person from the main goal and fundamental issues; "La'b" is the performance of an action, like a game, in which there is no serious intention.(158) "Hādhihi al-Ḥayātu ad-Dunyā" (this worldly life) is a symbol of the world's insignificance, just as "la-hiya al-Ḥayawān" (it is the life) is a symbol of the greatness of the Hereafter.

Question: Although the Quran itself recommends work and effort for cultivating the earth, traveling, benefiting from nature, spouses, beauties, foods, and earning income, how then does this Ayah state that the world is nothing but amusement and play (lahw and la'b)?

Answer: Enjoyments that are pursued for the sake of achieving sacred goals, using sacred methods and means, within the bounds of law, and while observing other conditions—all of these are the cultivation ground (farm) for the Hereafter. What is belittled and condemned in this Ayah, and what is introduced as amusement and play (lahw and la'b), are those instances where there is no sacred goal accompanying them and they contradict religious and legal standards.

158) Mufradāt al-Rāghib.

Messages

1- The creation of the world is purposeful and wise, but negligence of the Hereafter, worldliness, and being immersed in it is foolish. «لهو و لعب»

2- Silence in the face of people's self-satisfaction is not permissible everywhere; sometimes, the heedless must be warned with a stern call and a shout. «لهو و لعب»

3- In the method of propagation (Da'wah), whenever you negate something ugly, offer a good alternative. «انّ الدّار الآخرة»

4- The real life is the life of the Hereafter. «الدّار الآخرة لهى الحیوان»

5- People do not know the reality of the Hereafter, otherwise they would not become attached to the world. «لو كانوا یعلمون»

Ayah

فَإِذَا رَكِبُوا۟ فِى ٱلْفُلْكِ دَعَوُا۟ ٱللَّهَ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ ٱلدِّينَ فَلَمَّا نَجَّىٰهُمْ إِلَى ٱلْبَرِّ إِذَا هُمْ يُشْرِكُونَ (٦٥)
When they board the ship, they invoke Allah putting exclusive faith in Him, but when He delivers them to land, behold, they ascribe partners [to Him], (65)

Key Points

| Question: Materialists, in their analysis, raise the issue of fear as the root of faith, saying: The source of religion is fear. A person who is afraid seeks refuge in his mother in childhood, and in adulthood, he seeks refuge in an imaginary power called God. Does this Ayah, which states that some people call upon God when they feel danger and are drowning, not confirm their theory? Answer: This Ayah states that attention to God occurs during fear, not that the original existence of God is born out of fear. For example, when we see a dog and feel danger, we turn towards a stone (to throw); but this does not mean that the stone itself came into existence because of the dog. The existence of an eternal power in the universe is an innate matter (fitrah) that becomes the focus of human attention during calamities.

Messages

1- Fear removes the dust of negligence and awakens the God-seeking nature (fitrah). «فاذا ركبوا... دعوا اللّه...»

2- Faith should not be seasonal or temporary. «فلمّا نجّاهم... یشركون»

3- Time and place do not render the examples of the Quran obsolete. Sailing has existed throughout human history. «فاذا ركبوا فى الفلك...»

4- More important than sincerity (ikhlas) is preserving it. «مخلصین - اذا هم یشركون»

5- Sincere supplication is answered. «دعوا اللّه مخلصین... فلمّا نجّاهم» (The letter fa in «فلمّا» immediately following the supplication is a sign that the prayer was answered.)

6- Rescue from difficulties and calamities is a path toward gratitude (shukr), not polytheism (shirk). «اذا هم یشركون»

Ayah

لِيَكْفُرُوا۟ بِمَآ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُمْ وَلِيَتَمَتَّعُوا۟ ۖ فَسَوْفَ يَعْلَمُونَ (٦٦)
being ungrateful for what We have given them! Let them enjoy. Soon they will know! (66)

Key Points

The intent of "یكفروا" (they disbelieve) is ingratitude for the blessing (kufr al-ni'mah), because it appears alongside "آتیناهم" (We gave them), meaning the granting of blessings.

Messages

1- Polytheism (Shirk) is a type of ingratitude and denial of blessings (kufr al-ni'mah). «لیكفروا»

2- In this world, the path to prosperity is open to both the righteous and the unrighteous. «آتیناهم»

3- Do not be pleased with successes accompanied by disbelief and ingratitude, for they have a dire end. «لیكفروا... ولیتمّتعوا فسوف ...»

4- In education (or upbringing), warning (or threat) is also necessary. «فسوف یعلمون»

Ayah

أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ أَنَّا جَعَلْنَا حَرَمًا ءَامِنًا وَيُتَخَطَّفُ ٱلنَّاسُ مِنْ حَوْلِهِمْ ۚ أَفَبِٱلْبَـٰطِلِ يُؤْمِنُونَ وَبِنِعْمَةِ ٱللَّهِ يَكْفُرُونَ (٦۷)
Have they not seen that We have appointed a safe sanctuary, while the people are despoiled all around them? Would they then believe in falsehood and be ungrateful toward the blessing of Allah? (67)

Messages

1- One of the ways to invite people to Allah is by drawing their attention to His blessings. «أولم یروا»

2- Recalling the insecurities surrounding man makes security valuable to him. «یتخطّف الناس من حولهم»

3- Security is the foundation and context for worship; (159) but a group does not appreciate its value and turns towards disbelief. «حرماً آمناً ... أفبالباطل یؤمنون و بنعمة اللَّه یكفرون» (159) فلیعبدوا ربّ هذا البیت . الّذى ... آمنهم من خوف». Quraish, 3 - 4.

Ayah

وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ ٱفْتَرَىٰ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبًا أَوْ كَذَّبَ بِٱلْحَقِّ لَمَّا جَآءَهُۥٓ ۚ أَلَيْسَ فِى جَهَنَّمَ مَثْوًى لِّلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (٦۸)
Who is a greater wrongdoer than him who fabricates a lie against Allah, or denies the truth when it comes to him? Is not the [final] abode of the faithless in hell? (68)

Messages

1- Revelation must be accepted without addition or subtraction. Adding anything to the religion is slander (fabrication) and the worst injustice. «و من اظلم ممّن افترى»

2- The worst injustice is cultural injustice, which strikes a blow against man's submission to the Truth (Haqq). «ممّن افترى - او كذّب»

3- Divine punishment occurs after the completion of the argument (proof). «كذّب بالحقّ لما جائه أليس فى جهنّم...»

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ جَـٰهَدُوا۟ فِينَا لَنَهْدِيَنَّهُمْ سُبُلَنَا ۚ وَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَمَعَ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (٦۹)
As for those who strive in Us, We shall surely guide them in Our ways, and Allah is indeed with the virtuous. (69)

Messages

1- Effort is necessary to attain special Divine guidance, and man himself must take the first step. "جاهدوا - لنهدینّهم" (Strive - We will surely guide them), just as He says elsewhere: "اِن تنصروا اللَّه ینصركم" (If you support Allah, He will support you) (160).

2- Sometimes, one step and one moment of sincere effort lead to eternal guidance and salvation. ("جاهدوا" is in the past tense (imperative) and "لنهدینّهم" is in the continuous present tense.)

3- In management and leadership, it is not necessary for all dimensions of the work to be clear from the first day; as soon as a Divine step is taken, unseen guidance arrives along the way. "جاهدوا - لنهدینّهم"

4- What gives value to efforts is sincerity. "فینا" (in Our cause).

5- The paths of reaching Divine proximity have no limitation. "سبلنا" (Our paths).

6- We must be confident in Allah's promises. "لنهدینّهم" (The letter lām and the emphatic nūn are signs of emphasis).

7- The sign of being a doer of good is effort in the way of truth and sincerity. "جاهدوا فینا - لمع المحسنین"

8- The companionship of the Great God with a weak servant means man attains everything. "انّ اللّه لمع المحسنین" (Indeed, Allah is with the doers of good). As we read in the Du'a of 'Arafah by Imam Husayn عليه السلام: "ماذا فقد مَن وجدك و ماذا وجد مَن فقدك" (What has he lost who found You, and what has he found who lost You?). Whoever finds You, what deficiency does he have, and whoever loses You, what does he have?

9- Allah both shows us the path of proximity and holds our hand until we reach the destination. "لنهدینّهم... مع المحسنین"

(160) Muhammad, 7.
